channel.title: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism.
channel.description: KillBait is a news aggregator developed by students and professionals in journalism and web development from Argentina, Spain, and the United Kingdom. Our focus is on providing readers with clear, concise, and relevant summaries of each news story.

item.title: What is KillBait?
item.description: Surely you've clicked on a news article with an eye-catching or sensational headline...

item.title: How KillBait works
item.description: Our editors, along with any registered user, can submit news they find interesting simply by providing the URL to the system.

item.title: Upcoming news
DHS Inspector General Audits Warehouse-Based Immigration Detention Program Initiated by Kristi Noem
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security's inspector general has begun an audit of a $38 billion program that converted commercial warehouses into immigration detention centers. The initiative, led by former DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and her adviser Corey Lewandowski, involved the rapid purchase of 11 vacant warehouses during the winter of 2025, funded through a Republican-backed bill. Critics have raised concerns about the efficiency and legality of the program, citing purchases that exceeded market value by 11-13% and contractors with little or no prior experience in immigration detention. The audit will examine all acquisitions under this program and expand on prior investigations into DHS contract management and the influence of political appointees. Many lawmakers and officials questioned whether buying existing jail facilities would have been faster and less costly, as several warehouses lacked proper zoning and infrastructure for large detainee populations. Following Noem's dismissal

item.title: The Rise of Barbra Streisand: Iconic Portraits from the 1960s
item.description: Barbra Streisand, born in 1942, emerged as a groundbreaking singer, actress, and performer during the 1960s. This decade marked her transformation from a teenage nightclub singer to an international superstar. Streisand became widely recognized for her powerful, technically flawless voice and unique dramatic phrasing, treating every song like a mini theatrical performance. Her Broadway breakthrough came in 1962 with 'I Can Get It for You Wholesale,' followed by her iconic portrayal of Fanny Brice in 'Funny Girl' in 1964. Her CBS special 'My Name Is Barbra' in 1965 further demonstrated her ability to carry a major production alone. Streisand transitioned to Hollywood in 1968, starring in the film version of 'Funny Girl' and later in 'Hello, Dolly!' Her 1960s style was equally influential, featuring bold eyeliner, heavy lashes, sculptural beehives, and fashion-forward outfits by Arnold Scaasi. She embraced her distinctive looks and defied conventional beauty standards, becoming a symbol

item.title: Teen Sports Podcaster Seeks Guidance from Industry Professionals
item.description: A 16-year-old high school junior with a passion for sports broadcasting has started their own podcast in hopes of gaining experience and connections in the field. From the very beginning, the teenager has actively reached out to established sports professionals, seeking advice, interviews, and mentorship. Despite these efforts, many of the contacted professionals have not responded, leaving the young podcaster frustrated and disheartened. The teen expresses a desire for better communication within the industry, emphasizing that aspiring broadcasters could greatly benefit from guidance and encouragement from experienced professionals. This story highlights the challenges young people face when trying to break into competitive careers and the importance of mentorship and accessibility within professional communities. It also sheds light on the growing popularity of independent content creation, such as podcasts, as a way for youth to pursue their ambitions while building their skills and

item.title: Exploring the 1902 Arrol-Johnston 10/12hp Dogcart: Scotland’s Early Automotive Innovation
item.description: The 1902 Arrol-Johnston 10/12hp Dogcart represents a pioneering achievement in early Scottish automotive history. Created by George Johnston and funded by Sir William Arrol under the Mo-Car Syndicate Ltd., this vehicle showcased innovative design elements for its era. Its distinctive 'dogcart' body could accommodate six passengers with the driver seated in the middle row, reflecting a unique approach to vehicle layout. Powered by a twin-cylinder opposed-piston engine, the car could reach speeds of up to 25 miles per hour, a notable performance for the early 1900s. Built with a solid wooden frame and equipped with spoon-type brakes acting directly on the rear tires, the Dogcart was engineered to handle the rough, unpaved roads of its time. Surviving models today offer a window into the early days of British motoring, highlighting both the technical ingenuity and the adventurous spirit of early automobile pioneers. Enthusiasts and historians can explore the vehicle’s craftsmanship and hi

item.title: Analysis Shows Reading Skills Declining Nationwide, With L.A. and Select California Districts Making Gains
item.description: A new national study shows that U.S. students are experiencing a long-term decline in reading skills, a trend that started well before the pandemic. Harvard, Stanford, and Dartmouth researchers analyzed test scores from over 5,000 school districts in 38 states, revealing minimal progress in most regions. California ranked 29th in reading growth from 2022 to 2025, while math scores showed modest improvements. Nationally, students are nearly half a grade level behind pre-pandemic reading levels, whereas in California the gap is roughly a third of a grade. The decline has been ongoing since 2013 for eighth-graders and 2015 for fourth-graders. However, some districts, including Los Angeles Unified, Compton, and Modesto, are making notable progress. These districts have implemented phonics-based instruction as part of the 'science of reading' and provided targeted support for struggling students, leading to improvements in both reading and math scores. Los Angeles Unified, for example, has

item.title: Republicans increasingly defy Trump as his political leverage fades
item.description: An increasing number of Republican lawmakers are openly challenging President Donald Trump’s directives as his political leverage weakens. Historically, Trump has used the threat of supporting primary challengers to pressure GOP members into compliance. However, with the primary election season largely concluded nationwide, this influence has diminished until at least 2028. As a result, Republicans are more frequently resisting the president’s policy requests. Recent examples include skepticism over a $1 billion funding request for Trump’s White House ballroom project, House Speaker Mike Johnson publicly rejecting a housing-related bill Trump pushed, and South Carolina Republicans opposing efforts to redraw congressional district maps. Additionally, Senator Dan Sullivan of Alaska has sided with Democratic lawmakers on several votes despite Trump winning Alaska by a significant margin in 2024. While Trump recently leveraged influence in Indiana to remove dissenting state GOP lawmakers,

item.title: Congresswoman describes overcrowding and poor conditions at ICE detention center in Arizona
item.description: Congresswoman Adelita Grijalva (D-AZ) has described alarming conditions inside the Arizona Removal Operations Coordination Center in Mesa, Arizona, where Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detains immigrants before deportation. Grijalva recounted seeing severe overcrowding, with up to 47 detainees confined in spaces meant for 21, leaving no room for proper seating or airflow. She observed detainees without adequate food, enduring sweltering conditions, and cited this as the cause for an ongoing hunger strike she visited to investigate. Grijalva highlighted that the Mesa facility is the only location in the U.S. that allows families to remain together, which she found especially heartbreaking as she spoke with multiple families about their experiences. She also criticized staff behavior, describing them as hostile and largely uncommunicative, with very few speaking Spanish. Grijalva noted changes in staff interaction since Markwayne Mullin took over the Department of Homeland Sec

item.title: Michael J. Fox and Susanna Hoffs: A Brief 1986 Romance
item.description: In 1986, at the peak of their fame, actor Michael J. Fox and singer Susanna Hoffs of The Bangles shared a brief romantic relationship. This short-lived coupling occurred while Fox was enjoying massive success from the Back to the Future film series and Hoffs was gaining recognition as the lead singer of The Bangles, known for hits like 'Manic Monday.' Despite this notable pop culture connection, Fox has publicly stated that he does not remember dating Hoffs, attributing his memory loss to Parkinson’s disease, which he was diagnosed with in 1991. In a 2023 interview with The Sunday Times, Fox reflected on this period, acknowledging, 'I mean, I dated Susanna Hoffs from the Bangles, and I can’t even remember it.' After their brief relationship, Fox married his Family Ties co-star Tracy Pollan in 1988, and they have been together ever since. Hoffs went on to marry film director Jay Roach in 1993. This glimpse into 1980s celebrity life highlights a fleeting but memorable intersection of Hol

item.title: Women's Voluntary Service Collected Kitchen Waste for Pig Food in Britain During World War II
item.description: During World War II, Britain faced severe food shortages due to German U-boat attacks disrupting imports. To maximize domestic food production, particularly meat from pigs, the government launched salvage and recycling efforts on the Home Front. The Women’s Voluntary Service (WVS), now known as the Royal Voluntary Service, played a crucial role in these initiatives by collecting kitchen waste, often referred to as 'swill,' to be used as pig feed. Households were encouraged to save vegetable peelings, leftovers, and other scraps in pig bins, which could be individual or communal. WVS members organized collections, conducted door-to-door canvassing, and delivered the waste to farmers or specialized processing centers where it was boiled and sterilized. Over 42,000 WVS volunteers participated in various salvage activities, including this one. The effort helped reduce waste while supporting agriculture and the war effort. These historical photos from 1943 served as propaganda to boost publ

item.title: A Look Back at the 1984 Bicentennial Parade in Lebanon, Pennsylvania
item.description: The 1984 Bicentennial Parade in Lebanon, Pennsylvania, marked a significant milestone as the city celebrated its 200th anniversary. Drawing thousands of local residents and visitors, the parade transformed downtown Lebanon into a vibrant showcase of community pride and heritage. Participants included high school marching bands performing lively routines, vintage car enthusiasts displaying classic automobiles, and local businesses presenting elaborately decorated floats. The event highlighted Lebanon County’s rich industrial and agricultural history while creating a sense of unity among its citizens. The parade was not only a celebration of the past but also a hopeful glance toward the future, as families and friends gathered along the streets to commemorate two centuries of community life. Archival photographs from the parade reveal the colorful costumes, joyful expressions, and meticulous attention to detail that made the celebration memorable. For many, these images evoke nostalgia,

item.title: Mary Trump warns of threats to democracy following Virginia FBI raid and court ruling
item.description: Mary Trump, psychologist and political commentator, has raised concerns about recent actions she views as threats to democratic institutions. She highlighted the FBI raid on the office of L. Louise Lucas, the Democratic president pro tempore of the Virginia State Senate, alongside a ruling by the Virginia Supreme Court that struck down a voter-approved redistricting referendum. The referendum, supported by voters, would have benefited Democrats in four newly drawn districts, but the court ruled it unconstitutional on a technicality, giving Republicans an advantage. Mary Trump emphasized that these events are interconnected, reflecting what she calls 'democratic erosion.' She argued that while the judiciary undermines voter decisions, federal law enforcement is being used to intimidate politicians who oppose the administration's goals. Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche confirmed the FBI investigation but offered no further details, while media coverage, particularly by Fox News, fram

item.title: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke dies at age 29
item.description: Brandon Clarke, a professional basketball player for the NBA's Memphis Grizzlies, has tragically passed away at the age of 29. Clarke, known for his athleticism and defensive skills, played a significant role on the Grizzlies roster as a forward. Throughout his career, he gained recognition not only for his performance on the court but also for his dedication to his team and positive presence in the locker room. His sudden death has shocked the basketball community, fans, and teammates, who have shared their condolences and memories of his contributions both as a player and as a person. Clarke's journey in professional basketball began after his college career, where he demonstrated consistent growth and promise. While the exact circumstances surrounding his death have not been fully disclosed at this time, the news has prompted an outpouring of grief and tributes across social media and sports networks. His legacy as a talented and respected player will continue to be remembered by fa

item.title: Al Pacino's Immersive Experience Filming 'Serpico' (1973)
item.description: Al Pacino's work on the set of 'Serpico' (1973) demonstrated his deep dedication to the role of the titular plainclothes officer. The film employed a unique reverse chronological shooting schedule to accommodate Pacino's changing facial hair, beginning with a full beard and long hair and gradually trimming to a clean-shaven appearance. Director Sidney Lumet observed that Pacino remained in character throughout filming, adjusting his behavior to match the scene's mood. To fully inhabit his role, Pacino also explored New York City neighborhoods in character, at one point confronting a truck driver over exhaust pollution. He spent considerable time with the real Frank Serpico, even hosting him in Montauk, though the producer eventually requested Serpico avoid the set to prevent distractions. Filming was rushed, with only five months between start and release, yet the NYPD cooperated, impressed by Lumet’s commitment to authenticity. Shot entirely on location, the film captured New York’s r

item.title: Scientists develop method to detect sound from black hole mergers
item.description: Scientists have developed a groundbreaking technique that allows them to detect and 'hear' collisions between black holes. These cosmic events, which occur billions of light-years away, produce gravitational waves that ripple through space-time. By converting these waves into audible sound, researchers can study black hole mergers in unprecedented detail. This approach provides insights into the mass, spin, and dynamics of the colliding black holes. The technique relies on advanced computational models and data from gravitational wave observatories, offering a new dimension to astrophysical research. Experts believe this method could deepen our understanding of the formation and evolution of black holes, as well as the behavior of matter under extreme gravitational forces. While the sound itself is not something one could hear in space directly, translating gravitational signals into audio allows scientists to analyze patterns and frequencies that reveal critical information about thes

item.title: Moderna and Korea University Collaborate on Early-Stage Hantavirus mRNA Vaccine
item.description: Since 2023, Moderna and Korea University’s Vaccine Innovation Center (VIC-K) have been collaborating to develop an mRNA-based vaccine targeting hantavirus. This comes amid global awareness of hantavirus due to a recent outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship, which resulted in several serious cases and three deaths. Although the outbreak drew attention, Moderna had begun preclinical research years earlier. Hantavirus is transmitted primarily through contact with rodent excreta and can lead to severe diseases, including hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome in Asia and Europe or hantavirus pulmonary syndrome in the Americas. The Andean hantavirus (ANDV), identified in the MV Hondius outbreak, is notable for its high mortality rate and documented human-to-human transmission. Currently, no licensed vaccines exist outside South Korea, where an older vaccine, Hantavax, shows limited efficacy. Moderna’s approach leverages mRNA technology, proven effective during the COVID-19 pandemic, all

item.title: Chéri Hérouard and His Iconic Contributions to La Vie Parisienne
item.description: Chéri Hérouard (1881–1961) was a celebrated French illustrator whose work defined the Golden Age of French magazine art. Best known for his long-standing contributions to La Vie Parisienne, Hérouard’s illustrations spanned over four decades, showcasing his mastery of the Art Deco style. His work was renowned for its precise line work, elegance, and playful storytelling, often depicting ethereal women in elaborate costumes or whimsical, flirtatious scenes. Beyond fashion illustration, Hérouard captured the vibrant spirit and joie de vivre of early 20th-century France, blending glamour with a dreamlike, theatrical aesthetic. Many of his pieces, including covers and feature illustrations from the 1910s through the 1920s, remain highly collectible today. His ability to convey sophistication with charm made him a defining figure of his era, and his legacy continues to influence contemporary illustration and fashion art.

item.title: Canadian Soldiers Rescue Abandoned Baby in England During WWII
item.description: On September 23, 1941, a newborn baby was abandoned in Horsell Common, a park near London. Three Canadian soldiers—Gunner Robert C. Griffin from Regina, Saskatchewan, Lance Sergeant Ernest Curtis from Brandon, Manitoba, and Gunner Alonzo J. Brackett from Terrace, British Columbia—were nearby when they heard unusual noises from the bushes. They discovered the baby struggling to survive and immediately acted, cutting the remaining umbilical cord and wrapping her in a white shirt. The 8th Field Regiment's war diary recorded the event simply as 'found a newborn baby in the gun area.' The baby was taken to a nearby hospital and survived. She was later adopted by an English couple and named Mary, growing up unaware of her early life story. It wasn’t until 2018, at the age of 79, that Mary discovered a photograph of the soldiers holding her as a baby, revealing the truth of her rescue. Though the soldiers had passed away, she later contacted Harry Curtis, son of Lance Sergeant Curtis. Today,

item.title: The Life and Legacy of Soraya Esfandiary-Bakhtiary: Queen of Iran and European Socialite
item.description: Soraya Esfandiary-Bakhtiary (1932–2001) was the second wife of Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, serving as Queen of Iran from 1951 until their divorce in 1958. Her marriage, though initially celebrated, faced enormous pressure when it became clear that she could not bear children. In a decision influenced by dynastic responsibilities, she agreed to divorce to allow the Shah to produce an heir, making headlines worldwide as a deeply personal and national matter. Known for her beauty and style, Soraya became an international fashion icon, frequently appearing on magazine covers and drawing comparisons to Hollywood stars like Ava Gardner. After leaving Iran, she lived primarily in Rome and Paris, earning the title of Princess. In the 1960s, she explored a brief acting career, starring in Italian films such as I tre volti (The Three Faces) and She. During this period, she also had a relationship with Italian director Franco Indovina until his tragic death in a plane crash in 1972. Despite her g

item.title: The Life and Art of Charles Spencelayh: Victorian and Edwardian Genre Painter
item.description: Charles Spencelayh (1865–1958) was an English painter renowned for his meticulous genre paintings, capturing the intricate details of everyday life. Often referred to as a 'microscopic' realist, he specialized in portraying domestic interiors, frequently depicting elderly men surrounded by clocks, antiques, and personal curiosities. His works serve as intimate snapshots of the Victorian and Edwardian eras, combining technical precision with a gentle sense of humor and nostalgia. Each painting invites viewers to explore a rich narrative, where the smallest objects and gestures reveal human stories and personal histories. Some of his most notable works include 'The Old Dealer (The Old Curiosity Shop)', 'Time on His Hands', 'A Bargain', 'A Favourite Pet', and 'Patience Patience'. Spencelayh’s artistry highlights the beauty of the mundane, transforming ordinary rooms into detailed visual histories that celebrate memory, character, and the passage of time. His legacy endures as a testament

item.title: Shirley Anne Field: Celebrating the Legacy of a British Cinema Star
item.description: Shirley Anne Field (1936–2023) was a prominent figure in British cinema, particularly known for her influence during the 'New Wave' movement of the late 1950s and 1960s. Originally a successful photographic model, she transitioned into acting, gaining recognition for her remarkable beauty, charisma, and natural screen presence. Field starred in several iconic films that defined the era, including 'The Entertainer,' where she appeared alongside Laurence Olivier, and 'Saturday Night and Sunday Morning,' a cornerstone of British kitchen-sink realism. Her performances captured the evolving cultural and social landscape of post-war Britain, merging the allure of classic Hollywood glamour with the raw, authentic emotions of contemporary storytelling. Beyond her film roles, Field became a symbol of 1960s style and sophistication, embodying both elegance and modernity. Vintage portraits of her highlight the magnetic charm and timeless appeal that made her one of the most sought-after actresses

item.title: Researchers Identify Key Molecular Mechanism Used by Enteroviruses for Replication
item.description: Scientists at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County, have revealed a critical mechanism that enteroviruses, including polio and the common cold virus, use to replicate inside human cells. The study, published in Nature Communications, shows that viral RNA functions as a molecular 'switch' to control whether the virus produces proteins or copies its genome. This process involves a viral fusion protein, 3CD, which recruits both viral and host proteins to assemble the replication machinery. Researchers used advanced techniques such as X-ray crystallography, isothermal titration calorimetry, and biolayer interferometry to capture the interactions between viral RNA and proteins in unprecedented detail. The findings also show that two 3CD molecules are required for effective replication, providing new insight into viral behavior. Notably, the RNA structures and binding behaviors are highly conserved across seven enteroviruses, highlighting a potential target for broad-spectrum antivir

item.title: Trump Administration Pursues Aggressive Voter Roll Purges Ahead of 2026 Elections
item.description: According to reporting highlighted by Raw Story, the Trump administration is undertaking a nationwide effort to remove tens of millions of voters from registration rolls, targeting primarily Democratic-leaning citizens. This move follows historical GOP strategies, including previous purges in Florida during the 2000 election and practices like 'caging' and strict signature matching that disproportionately affect Black, young, and urban voters. The Department of Justice has requested comprehensive voter data from multiple states and is pressuring compliance via Memoranda of Understanding. Some Republican-led states are cooperating, while around 30 Democratic-led states face lawsuits for resisting federal pressure. Experts warn that these purges, combined with voter ID laws, polling place manipulations, and intimidation tactics, are designed to suppress votes in key areas, similar to strategies seen in other authoritarian contexts. Reports also indicate that millions of eligible voters w

item.title: xAI Expands Gas Turbine Use at Southaven Data Center Amid Environmental Lawsuit
item.description: xAI has installed 19 additional portable natural gas turbines at its Colossus 2 data center in Southaven, Mississippi, raising concerns over air quality and regulatory compliance. Internal emails obtained by WIRED reveal that these turbines were added between late March and early May, increasing the total number of turbines at the site to 46 and adding over 500 megawatts of capacity. The company is currently facing a lawsuit from the NAACP, the Southern Environmental Law Center, and Earthjustice, alleging violations of the Clean Air Act due to operating 27 turbines without proper permits. Some turbines were installed even after the lawsuit was filed, intensifying legal scrutiny. Regulators note that temporary turbines can operate for up to a year without permits, but the NAACP has sought an emergency injunction to halt operations. xAI defends the turbines as essential for powering AI and government computing projects. The expansion mirrors controversies at xAI's first Colossus site in

item.title: A Look Back at Camilla Horn’s Career Through 1920s and 1930s Postcards
item.description: Camilla Horn (1903–1996) was a celebrated German actress and dancer, known for her transition from silent films to sound cinema, achieving success both in Hollywood and Europe. Discovered by renowned director F.W. Murnau, Horn first gained international fame for her role as Gretchen in the silent film Faust (1926), after serving as a stand-in for Lil Dagover. Her angelic appearance helped her secure major roles in Hollywood, including starring alongside John Barrymore in Tempest (1928) and Eternal Love (1929). Unlike many silent film stars, she maintained popularity during the sound era, later returning to European cinema with notable films such as The Great Desire (1930) and Schloß Königswald (1988). Horn’s life off-screen was equally dramatic; she married four times, maintained an independent spirit, and faced legal troubles during the Nazi era, reportedly resisting strict party conformity. Her autobiography recounts her interactions with historical figures, including Joseph Goebbels

item.title: French Woman Critically Ill from Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
item.description: A French woman infected during a hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship is critically ill and receiving life-support treatment at Bichat Hospital in Paris. The patient is on an artificial lung device to help her lungs and heart recover from the severe form of the disease. The outbreak has resulted in 11 reported cases so far, with nine confirmed infections and three deaths, including a Dutch couple believed to have first contracted the virus in South America. Authorities report that the virus, primarily spread through rodents, has rarely been transmitted between people, although the strain involved in this outbreak—the Andes virus—may allow limited human-to-human transmission. The World Health Organization confirmed that all cases are linked to the cruise ship and that no broader outbreak has been detected yet. Evacuated passengers and crew are under quarantine, with monitoring periods of up to 42 days advised by WHO. Spain, the Netherlands, and other countries involved are

item.title: Nintendo Announces Release of Mineru's Construct Amiibo for Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
item.description: Nintendo has officially announced the release of the 'Mineru's Construct' amiibo for The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom. Pre-orders are now available on Nintendo's US store for $34.99, and the amiibo is set to launch on September 17th, 2026. This figure represents Mineru, one of the Sages from the game, who assists Link through a Zonai construct. The collectible features articulated arms that can be posed, adding a unique display option for fans. In-game, tapping the amiibo unlocks random items across multiple Zelda games including Hyrule Warriors: Age of Imprisonment, Breath of the Wild, and Tears of the Kingdom, with a special paraglider fabric included for the latest title. The release comes as part of Nintendo's continued support for its amiibo line, even for games that have been out for several years. Fans of the series have expressed mixed reactions online, with some excited to add a new articulated figure to their collection, while others question Mineru’s usefulness or c

item.title: Emmet Gowin: Four Decades of Family Photography and Intimate Portraits
item.description: Emmet Gowin’s photographic collection, Baldwin Street: Photographs 1966-1994, showcases his deep commitment to capturing the intimate moments of family life over four decades. Published alongside his exhibition at Pace Gallery, the book draws its name from the street where four generations of his wife Edith’s family lived in Danville, Virginia. Gowin’s approach to photography is described as a form of devotion, characterized by attention, empathy, and care. Influenced by his Quaker upbringing and artistic training at the Richmond Professional Institute and Rhode Island School of Design under Harry Callahan, Gowin’s work blends formal elegance with emotional depth. Notable images include Reva and Edith, Danville, 1984, highlighting the enduring bond between mother and daughter, and Collecting Figs, Pastor Gowin, Ocean View, Virginia, 1976, a tender portrait of his father. Through compositions that explore line, form, balance, and rhythm, Gowin captures ordinary life with poetic vision,

item.title: The Last Drink of Outlaw John Shaw After His Death
item.description: In the early morning of April 8, 1905, John Shaw and his partner William Evans, also known as Bill Smythe, robbed the Wigwam Saloon in Winslow, Arizona, taking at least $200 in silver coins. The two young men, dressed sharply in suits, initially left their drinks untouched at the bar and focused on a dice game before fleeing with the money. Navajo County Sheriff Chet Houck, along with Deputy Pete Pemberton, pursued them and confronted the pair near Canyon Diablo. During a tense shootout, Shaw ran out of bullets and was fatally shot in the head, while Evans was wounded and later imprisoned for nine years. Shaw’s body was buried in a shallow grave, but the story took an unusual turn when a group of cowboys, upon hearing of his unfinished drink, exhumed his body to pour whiskey into his mouth as a final toast. Six photographs captured this strange event, though only four are currently held by the Arizona Historical Society, with the remaining images lost over time. This incident remains o

item.title: Nedra Talley, Last Original Ronettes Member, Passes Away at 80
item.description: Nedra Talley-Ross (1946–2026), the final surviving original member of the iconic 1960s girl group The Ronettes, passed away on April 26, 2026, at age 80. Her daughter confirmed that Talley-Ross died peacefully at home surrounded by family. As a founding member alongside her cousins Ronnie Spector and Estelle Bennett, Talley-Ross helped define the 'Wall of Sound' era with timeless hits like 'Be My Baby,' 'Baby, I Love You,' and 'Walking in the Rain.' The Ronettes were trailblazers, breaking racial barriers as young women of Puerto Rican and Black heritage performing on mainstream television, and they were the only girl group to tour with The Beatles in their final 1966 U.S. tour. After leaving the group in 1967, Talley-Ross became a born-again Christian and released the contemporary Christian album 'Full Circle' in 1978. Later in life, she pursued a career in real estate in Virginia Beach. Her passing closes a chapter in music history, following the earlier deaths of Estelle Bennett in

item.title: Hanoi Sidewalk Air-Raid Shelters During the 1967 Vietnam War
item.description: This photograph, taken in 1967 by American photojournalist Lee Lockwood for Life magazine, captures the daily life of Hanoi residents during the Vietnam War. The image shows individuals sheltering in narrow, chest-deep concrete bunkers dug into the sidewalks, often referred to as 'turtle-shell' shelters. These one-person shelters were a key part of Hanoi's civil defense during Operation Rolling Thunder, the U.S. bombing campaign against North Vietnam. Staggered every 20 meters along major streets, the bunkers allowed residents to quickly find safety when air-raid sirens sounded, which occurred multiple times daily. Each shelter was equipped with a lid made of concrete, bamboo, or straw to protect against shrapnel and debris. People would remain inside until the all-clear signal was given. These sidewalk bunkers became an iconic symbol of civilian resilience in Hanoi and were largely removed after the Paris Peace Accords in 1973. The image not only documents the hardships and dangers fa

item.title: Former DOJ Prosecutor Highlights Possible Vindictive Actions in Trump’s Case Against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: A former federal prosecutor, Glenn Kirschner, has raised concerns that the Trump administration's prosecution of Kilmar Abrego Garcia could be undermined due to selective and potentially vindictive treatment. Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man wrongfully deported to El Salvador, was targeted for deportation to Liberia despite not having any criminal convictions, a practice inconsistent with how other Liberians with criminal records have been treated. Kirschner discussed on his podcast 'Justice Matters' that such discrepancies strengthen the argument that the prosecution may be retaliatory. The case traces back to a 2022 traffic stop in Tennessee, where Abrego Garcia was issued only a warning despite carrying nine passengers. The charges only resurfaced after his wrongful deportation, which was later reversed following a Supreme Court ruling. Two federal judges are currently evaluating whether the prosecution constitutes selective or vindictive action. Judge Waverly Crenshaw in Tennessee has

item.title: Ann-Margret’s Career and Style Highlights in the 1970s
item.description: Ann-Margret Olsson, widely known as Ann-Margret, is an iconic American actress, dancer, and singer whose career spans seven decades. During the 1970s, she transitioned from a musical performer to a critically acclaimed dramatic actress, redefining her public image. Her role as Bobbie in Carnal Knowledge (1971) earned her an Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actress, marking a turning point in her career. In 1975, she appeared in the film adaptation of the rock opera Tommy, receiving her second Oscar nomination and winning a Golden Globe. Despite suffering a serious accident in September 1972, falling 22 feet during a performance in Lake Tahoe and sustaining multiple injuries, she made a remarkable comeback after extensive surgery and recovery. Her resilience helped her overcome stage fright and strengthen her connection with fans. Throughout the decade, Ann-Margret became a symbol of retro glamour, often wearing bold gowns and dramatic makeup that influenced 1970s fashion. S

item.title: Behind the Scenes: Iconic Photos from 'A Night at the Opera' (1935)
item.description: Released in November 1935, 'A Night at the Opera' is considered one of the Marx Brothers' most celebrated films and their first project with Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer. Directed by Sam Wood, the film marked a turning point for the comedy team, being their first without Zeppo Marx and the first to feature a more structured narrative, guided by producer Irving Thalberg. The plot follows brothers Groucho, Chico, and Harpo Marx as they help struggling opera singers Ricardo and Rosa achieve success, while contending with a pompous opera director and navigating adventures from Italy to New York. Famous for iconic comedy sequences, the film includes a classic cabin crowding scene and a chaotic orchestra pit baseball game. Thalberg encouraged the inclusion of a romantic subplot and musical interludes, broadening the film's appeal. The Marx Brothers tested the script on a vaudeville tour to ensure timing and audience reaction were perfect. 'A Night at the Opera' became a commercial hit and was preserv

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Jennifer Jones in Duel in the Sun (1946)
item.description: This article showcases a collection of 40 vintage portraits of actress Jennifer Jones on the set of the 1946 Technicolor film “Duel in the Sun.” The images capture Jones in her transformative role as Pearl Chavez, highlighting both her dramatic acting range and striking sun-kissed beauty. Produced by David O. Selznick, the film was a high-budget, controversial epic of its time, often referred to as a “super-western.” The photos provide a behind-the-scenes glimpse of the rigorous yet glamorous filmmaking process, including costume design, makeup, and on-set interactions. Dressed in her iconic Western attire, Jones embodies the fiery and tragic character central to the story. Beyond showcasing the actress’s performance, the portraits serve as a historical window into 1940s Hollywood, reflecting the era’s production style, artistic ambitions, and cultural attitudes toward epic cinema. These images appeal to film enthusiasts, vintage photography collectors, and anyone interested in the Gol

item.title: Trump Administration Outlines Counterterrorism Strategy Targeting Domestic Political Groups
item.description: The Trump administration released its 2026 U.S. Counterterrorism Strategy, focusing on what it calls 'violent secular political groups.' According to the strategy, the administration plans to prioritize the identification and neutralization of domestic groups whose ideologies are described as anti-American, radically pro-transgender, and anarchist. The strategy outlines measures including mapping group memberships, investigating connections with international organizations such as Antifa, and using law enforcement tools to disrupt these groups before they can harm civilians. By positioning secular and transgender-rights groups alongside organized crime and Islamist terrorist organizations, the plan has drawn significant attention for its broad categorization of domestic political activists as potential threats. President Trump emphasized that this approach is part of his commitment to defending America from all enemies, both foreign and domestic. The strategy also highlights the admini

item.title: Man Injured in Altercation with Teen E-Bike Riders in Huntington Beach
item.description: A man in Huntington Beach was injured over the weekend after an altercation with a large group of teenagers riding electric bikes near the beach. According to Sam El-Said, he and his wife were riding scooters when someone in the crowd threw a glass bottle at him, and he was subsequently kicked and stomped by several individuals. One minor has been arrested and cited for misdemeanor battery. Huntington Beach police continue to investigate the incident. The city has become a popular spot for teen e-bike gatherings, often coordinated through social media, sometimes involving hundreds of riders. The day before the attack, the police conducted a crackdown on illegal e-bikes and electric motorcycles, issuing citations, warnings, and even impounding one vehicle. California law classifies e-bikes into three classes based on speed and motor power, with additional rules for electric motorcycles. The police department emphasized its commitment to safety through enforcement, education, and communi

item.title: Transformations of Early 20th Century New York City
item.description: New York City in the early 1900s was undergoing a remarkable transformation, positioning itself as a symbol of modernity and ambition at the dawn of the American Century. The city skyline began to change dramatically with the emergence of the first skyscrapers, including iconic structures like the Flatiron Building and the St. Paul Building, which were once among the tallest in the world. The streets were alive with a mixture of horse-drawn carriages, early automobiles, and streetcars, reflecting both chaos and energy. Immigrant neighborhoods like the Lower East Side showcased the grit and determination of new arrivals, while areas such as Fifth Avenue displayed opulence and social prestige. The period also saw the rise of cultural landmarks, including Broadway theaters and entertainment destinations like Coney Island's Dreamland. Infrastructure projects such as the New York City Subway and the Williamsburg Bridge further demonstrated the city's rapid modernization. Photographs from th

item.title: A Glimpse of Venice in 1954 Through Vintage Photography
item.description: In 1954, Venice retained its status as one of the most captivating cities in the world. Largely untouched by modern development, the city exuded a serene and graceful charm, with gondolas gliding through quiet canals and the gentle ripple of water against historic stone walls. This period attracted a refined audience of artists, writers, filmmakers, and affluent travelers who were drawn to the city’s romantic and nostalgic atmosphere. Grand hotels along the Grand Canal and fashionable crowds in Piazza San Marco reflected the lingering elegance of a bygone era. The city’s palaces, though showing signs of age, added a melancholic beauty that photographers and cineastes sought to capture. This collection of vintage photos offers a window into Venice’s mid-20th century life, showcasing iconic locations such as the Grand Canal, Piazza San Marco, Ponte dell'Accademia, and Chiesa di San Vidal. From street scenes to quiet moments in cafes, these images preserve the cultural essence and timeles

item.title: David Venturella, former private prison executive, appointed as ICE director
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has appointed David Venturella, a former executive at the private prison company Geo Group, as the new director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Venturella's appointment comes at a time when ICE faces heightened scrutiny over its role in enforcing immigration policies, particularly those associated with mass detention and deportation programs implemented under the Trump administration. As director, Venturella will oversee the agency’s operations, including detention facilities, enforcement strategies, and coordination with federal and state authorities. His background in the private prison sector has sparked concerns among immigration advocates who argue that privatized detention management may prioritize profit over humane treatment. Supporters contend that Venturella brings extensive experience in operational management, which could strengthen ICE’s capacity to carry out its responsibilities effectively. The decision is expe

item.title: Life and Culture in France During the Mid-1950s
item.description: The article offers a vivid glimpse into life in France during the mid-1950s, a period known as the Trente Glorieuses or 'Thirty Glorious Years.' Post-war France experienced a mix of modernization and traditional charm. In Paris, streets were alive with the aroma of fresh baguettes, the sounds of jazz from basement clubs, and intellectual conversations about existentialism in Left Bank cafes. The countryside maintained a slower, picturesque lifestyle, where horse-drawn carts coexisted with the iconic Citroën 2CV. The era also saw cultural highlights such as the rise of Christian Dior's 'New Look' fashion and simple pleasures like family picnics along the Seine. The article is accompanied by numerous vintage photographs capturing landmarks, streets, and daily life across cities like Dijon, Beaune, and Paris, as well as rural locations, offering a nostalgic view of elegance, resilience, and the celebrated joie de vivre of mid-20th century France.

item.title: Christiane Amanpour Expresses Concerns Over Potential Changes at CNN Under David Ellison Ownership
item.description: Veteran CNN journalist Christiane Amanpour expressed concerns about the future of her network during a journalism summit in London. Speaking at the Truth Tellers Sir Harry Evans Investigative Journalism Summit, Amanpour highlighted worries regarding incoming ownership changes tied to David Ellison's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery, the parent company of CNN. She drew parallels to ongoing shifts at CBS News under Ellison's influence through Paramount and Skydance. Amanpour noted criticism from figures like Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and described CNN as a target for the current administration. She voiced personal concern based on observed changes at CBS, including declining viewership, financial losses, and what she termed an 'ideological realignment' that could impact the legacy program '60 Minutes'. Amanpour praised '60 Minutes' as a longstanding pillar of American television journalism delivering hard news and cultural stories. She emphasized the importance of maintaining e

item.title: A Look at Stanley Kubrick’s Early Film ‘Killer’s Kiss’ Through 30 Iconic Photos
item.description: Stanley Kubrick’s 1955 film 'Killer’s Kiss' offers an early glimpse into the legendary director’s visual style and filmmaking ingenuity. This American film noir, lasting only 67 minutes, was Kubrick’s second feature-length production and was made on a modest budget of approximately $75,000. The movie stars Jamie Smith, Irene Kane (journalist Chris Chase), and Frank Silvera, with Kubrick’s wife, Ruth Sobotka, contributing both as a small-acting role and as art director. Kubrick took on multiple key roles in the production, functioning as director, cinematographer, and editor, while also managing post-production dubbing due to a lack of synchronized sound equipment. One of the film’s most memorable moments is the surreal warehouse shootout featuring mannequins, which highlights Kubrick’s early fascination with lighting, shadows, and uncanny visuals. Beyond its technical achievements, 'Killer’s Kiss' captures authentic 1950s New York, showcasing locations like the original Penn Station an

item.title: Reflections on History and the Tango 'Cambalache'
item.description: In this blog post dated May 12, 2026, Juan Manuel Grijalvo reflects on history, human behavior, and the famous tango 'Cambalache.' The author discusses popular sayings such as 'I forgive but I do not forget' and the often misattributed quote to Mark Twain, 'History does not repeat itself, but it rhymes,' exploring the cyclical nature of human events. Grijalvo shares the lyrics of 'Cambalache,' a tango by Argentine composer Enrique Santos Discépolo, highlighting the persistent social inequalities, moral ambiguities, and chaos that remain constant across centuries. The post emphasizes how in every era, the virtuous and the corrupt coexist, and distinctions between right and wrong often blur. Through comments from readers, the discussion extends to the metaphorical connection between life and tango, with the commenter 1027a noting that life itself resembles the chaotic mix described in 'Cambalache.' The article provides both historical reflection and cultural commentary, using the tango a

item.title: Drew Baglino, ex-Tesla executive, starts Sadi Thermal Machines to develop advanced heat pumps
item.description: Drew Baglino, a former senior executive at Tesla and the current CEO of Heron Power, has founded a new startup called Sadi Thermal Machines, focusing on heat pump technology. Established in June 2025, Sadi shares its headquarters in Scotts Valley, California, with Heron Power, which specializes in solid-state transformers. Baglino, who spent nearly two decades at Tesla, contributed to a range of projects including the Roadster, Powerwall, Powerpack, and Tesla's thermal management systems. Notably, he helped develop Tesla's patented thermal system with multiple coolant loops and valves, forming the foundation of the company’s octovalve technology used in the Model Y. The heat pump manages the temperature of the cabin, battery, and motors in a compact system. While Tesla has explored residential heat pumps in the past, it has not released a consumer HVAC or water heating product. Sadi Thermal Machines is expected to apply Baglino’s experience to create heat pumps for residential and comm

item.title: House Democrat Accuses DOJ of Approving Payments to FBI Agents with Misconduct Records
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin, the top Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, published a letter accusing the Trump-era Department of Justice of ordering payments to FBI agents with histories of misconduct, including involvement in the January 6 Capitol attack and associations with extremist groups. According to Raskin, Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche oversaw decisions by the DOJ Office of the Deputy Attorney General to reinstate and pay nearly a dozen agents despite evidence of criminal activity, ethical violations, and security breaches. Payments included lump sums and backpay, with one agent receiving $63,500 and another over $61,000. Specific cases cited include an agent present during the Capitol riots, one who illegally recorded FBI communications and shared documents with foreign media, and another who ignored violent white nationalist activity while engaging in illicit personal conduct on assignment. Raskin criticized Vance Day, the DOJ official managing these settlements, fo

item.title: 1962 Alfa Romeo Giulietta Sprint Speciale: Icon of Italian Automotive Design
item.description: The 1962 Alfa Romeo Giulietta Sprint Speciale stands as an extraordinary example of Italian automotive design and innovation from the early 1960s. Designed by Franco Scaglione for Bertone, the car’s sleek 'Low Drag' form draws inspiration from the B.A.T. concept cars, resulting in a fluid, futuristic silhouette that remains striking even today. Its handcrafted aluminum body is paired with a 1.3-liter twin-cam engine, offering a lively driving experience that balances performance and style. This model exemplifies an era when cars were celebrated as moving works of art rather than mere transportation. Collectors and enthusiasts continue to admire the Giulietta Sprint Speciale for its aerodynamic elegance and historical significance in automotive design. These photos showcase the timeless beauty and sculptural quality that make this Alfa Romeo a classic on the road.

item.title: Sam Altman Testifies in Musk Lawsuit Over OpenAI's Corporate Decisions
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman testified in court defending himself against a lawsuit from former co-founder Elon Musk, who is challenging OpenAI's corporate structure. Musk claimed that the creation of a for-profit subsidiary to commercialize AI products amounted to a misuse of the organization's charitable foundation. Altman rejected this framing, emphasizing that OpenAI's foundation is among the world's largest charitable organizations and continues to expand its philanthropic efforts. Testimony revealed tensions around AI safety during OpenAI’s early funding discussions. Altman recounted a concerning moment when Musk suggested that control of a for-profit OpenAI might pass to his children if he were to die. Altman argued this was incompatible with OpenAI’s mission to prevent concentrated control over advanced AI. He also criticized Musk’s management style, which he said demotivated key researchers through practices like ranking employee performance and eliminating roles. After disputes over

item.title: North Carolina Resident Evacuated for Monitoring After Hantavirus Exposure on Cruise Ship
item.description: A North Carolina resident is among the Americans who were evacuated to Nebraska for monitoring following exposure to a deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship. The North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services (NCDHHS) confirmed that the individual is now at the National Quarantine Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center in Omaha. The identity of the person has not been released. Health officials emphasize that there are no confirmed hantavirus cases in North Carolina, and the risk to the public remains extremely low. Passengers from the affected cruise ship will undergo a 42-day monitoring period for potential symptoms. Dr. Kelly Kimple, Director of the NCDHHS Division of Public Health, stated that the public health team is fully prepared to respond and coordinate with federal, state, and local authorities to ensure the safety of residents. Officials reiterated that the likelihood of infection for North Carolinians is 'very, very low,' and there is no immediate

item.title: Medicare launches AI-focused payment program to improve chronic care outcomes
item.description: Medicare has introduced ACCESS (Advancing Chronic Care with Effective, Scalable Solutions), a 10-year pilot program designed to test AI-driven healthcare at a federal level. Unlike traditional Medicare, which reimburses based on time spent with clinicians, ACCESS rewards health outcomes for chronic conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, chronic kidney disease, obesity, depression, and anxiety. Participants, including startups like Pair Team, receive predictable payments and only earn full reimbursement when measurable health goals are achieved. Pair Team, which has focused on patients with chronic conditions who also face social challenges like unstable housing and food insecurity, uses a voice AI agent called Flora to handle intake, coordinate referrals, and maintain engagement between visits. Flora provides companionship and regular monitoring, which has been shown to significantly reduce avoidable emergency and hospital visits. ACCESS was designed by former startup founders with

item.title: Joan Friedman and Her Iconic Influence on 1950s Fashion
item.description: Joan Friedman (1931–2014) was one of the most prominent figures of 1950s fashion, epitomizing elegance and sophistication during the era. As a high-fashion model, she worked with renowned photographers such as Horst P. Horst, Richard Rutledge, and Irving Penn, and appeared in elite publications including Vogue and Harper's Bazaar. Friedman's refined features, graceful poise, and ability to embody structured silhouettes made her a symbol of mid-century glamour. She modeled a wide array of iconic designs, from dramatic ballgowns and chic Dior-inspired suits to tailored tweed outfits and hand-woven dresses. Her appearances showcased the craftsmanship of celebrated designers such as Mainbocher, Ceil Chapman, David Crystal, and Bonnie Cashin. Joan’s photographs captured the essence of the 1950s fashion world, where every outfit and pose reflected elegance, sophistication, and high-society style. Today, her work continues to be a reference point for fashion historians and enthusiasts, repres

item.title: Uma Thurman's Iconic Appearances on Vogue Covers
item.description: Uma Thurman has had a distinguished and multifaceted career, not only as a celebrated actress but also as a fashion icon, with numerous appearances on Vogue magazine covers. Her journey began in her teenage years with early covers in British Vogue during the mid-1980s, photographed by Patrick Demarchelier. Throughout the 1990s, she appeared on international editions including Vogue UK, Brasil, Paris, Taiwan, and Korea. Her 1997 US Vogue cover coincided with her rising Hollywood fame and featured an interview titled 'Beautiful Girl' discussing beauty and personal relationships. In November 2003, Annie Leibovitz photographed Thurman for US Vogue, highlighting her transformative year, intense training, and personal growth. She continued to feature in Vogue through 2006 and 2008, with British Vogue's 'Ageless Style' issue emphasizing her timeless fashion and beauty. After a 14-year hiatus from magazine covers, Thurman made a celebrated return on the March 2022 issue of Vogue España, photog

item.title: The Life and Art of Tilly Losch: A Jazz Age Icon
item.description: Tilly Losch (1903–1975) was an Austrian-born dancer, actress, and painter whose career embodied the glamour and artistic innovation of the early 20th century. Renowned for her ethereal beauty and fluid, graceful movements, she began as a prima ballerina in Vienna and later captivated audiences on the stages of London and New York. Losch collaborated with influential figures such as Max Reinhardt and George Balanchine, starring in prominent productions including The Miracle and The Seven Deadly Sins. Beyond dance, she became a cultural icon, navigating high-profile marriages and ultimately shifting her creative focus to surrealist painting. Her life reflects a continual reinvention of self and a commitment to artistic expression that transcended traditional boundaries. These vintage photos illustrate her magnetic presence and the elegance she carried from stage to canvas, offering a glimpse into the style, sophistication, and avant-garde energy of the Jazz Age and Art Deco periods.

item.title: Penélope Cruz Reflects on Her Challenging Debut in 'Jamón Jamón' at Age 17
item.description: Penélope Cruz was only 17 when she filmed her first feature, 'Jamón Jamón' (1992), which would later make her a Spanish icon. Despite the movie's success and its role in establishing her as an international sex symbol, Cruz has described the experience as emotionally overwhelming. She admitted feeling unprepared for the explicit scenes, particularly her first nude scenes, and feared they could end her career before it began. On the last day of shooting in the harsh Los Monegros Desert in Aragon, Spain, Cruz broke down in tears, uncertain if she would ever act again. The intense emotional strain led her to change her appearance by cutting her hair short and avoiding romantic or intimate scenes for years. Nevertheless, she acknowledged that the film crew and co-stars, including Javier Bardem, were respectful of her age and the difficult nature of the material. Director Bigas Luna fostered a spontaneous atmosphere to capture raw performances. Interestingly, Cruz met Bardem on this set; al

item.title: Researchers Discover How Plants Produce the Cancer-Fighting Compound Mitraphylline
item.description: Researchers at the University of British Columbia Okanagan have unveiled the biological process by which certain tropical plants produce mitraphylline, a rare natural compound with potential anti-cancer properties. Mitraphylline belongs to the spirooxindole alkaloid class, known for their complex twisted structures and significant biological activity, including anti-inflammatory and anti-tumor effects. The team identified two key enzymes that orchestrate the molecule’s formation: one shapes the compound into its three-dimensional spiro structure, while the other completes its conversion into mitraphylline. Previously, the scarcity of this compound in plants like kratom and cat's claw made laboratory production difficult and unsustainable. This discovery opens the door to producing mitraphylline and related compounds in a more environmentally friendly way, potentially accelerating pharmaceutical research and drug development. The study was led by Dr. Thu-Thuy Dang and doctoral student T

item.title: The Life and Work of Irene of New York, Mid-Century Milliner
item.description: Irene of New York, born Irene Stone, was a prominent American milliner whose designs shaped Manhattan fashion from the 1940s through the 1960s. Operating her boutique on East 54th Street, she gained recognition for high-end, elegant headwear, sold at top luxury stores like Saks Fifth Avenue and Henri Bendel. Alongside her design partner H. Robert Greene, Irene was known for creating stylish yet wearable hats that balanced sophistication with practicality. Her innovative use of materials such as ciré-finished organza, tailored silk turbans, and floral garlands set her apart from contemporaries who often favored more theatrical styles. Irene's creations became iconic in fashion photography, frequently featured in Vogue, Harper's Bazaar, and Ladies' Home Journal, styled on models like Evelyn Tripp, Jessica Ford, and Simone d'Aillencourt. These designs not only highlighted her craftsmanship but also reflected the era’s attention to detail and the importance of hats as a symbol of elegance.

item.title: Behind the Scenes: Michelle Pfeiffer’s Role as Isabeau in Ladyhawke (1985)
item.description: Michelle Pfeiffer's portrayal of Isabeau d’Anjou in the 1985 fantasy film Ladyhawke marked a pivotal moment in her career, solidifying her status as a Hollywood icon. Initially hesitant to take on the role due to concerns about playing a typical 'damsel in distress,' Pfeiffer’s audition impressed director Richard Donner with her wit and sense of humor, including a playful 'hawk mimicry' that demonstrated her range. Filming took place across central and northern Italy, in breathtaking yet physically demanding locations such as the Rocca Calascio fortress and the Dolomites. Pfeiffer often performed during the 'golden hour' to achieve the film's distinctive lighting, coordinated by famed cinematographer Vittorio Storaro. The production required maintaining a sense of chemistry with Rutger Hauer, who replaced Kurt Russell as Navarre, despite their limited shared screen time due to the film's central curse plot. Pfeiffer also worked closely with trained red-tailed hawks, cultivating respect

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Photos of Kirsten Dunst on the Set of The Virgin Suicides
item.description: This article presents a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs of Kirsten Dunst during the filming of Sofia Coppola's 1999 film, The Virgin Suicides. At the time, Dunst was 16 years old while filming in Toronto and celebrated her 17th birthday shortly before the movie premiered at the Cannes Film Festival. The article also includes insights from both Dunst and Coppola. Coppola highlights Dunst's ability to convey depth behind her seemingly all-American appearance, praising their immediate creative connection. Dunst recalls her initial nervousness about her role, which involved romantic scenes with co-star Josh Hartnett, but also reflects on the fun and memorable experiences on set, such as complicated kissing scenes and playful mishaps. The article celebrates the film as one of Dunst's most memorable performances and emphasizes her natural charm and talent during her teenage years. Additionally, it provides context about the lasting collaboration between Coppola and Dunst on later

item.title: A Look at Tammi Terrell's Life and Career in the 1960s
item.description: Tammi Terrell, born Thomasina Winifred Montgomery on April 29, 1945, in Philadelphia, was a celebrated American singer best known for her work with Motown Records during the 1960s. She demonstrated musical talent early, singing in church and winning local talent contests. By her early teens, she performed in clubs, opening for acts like Patti LaBelle & the Bluebelles, and began recording professionally under the name Tammy Montgomery. She toured with James Brown’s Revue and briefly attended the University of Pennsylvania before dedicating herself fully to music. In 1965, she joined Motown after being discovered by Berry Gordy. While her early solo singles achieved modest success, Terrell’s fame skyrocketed through her duets with Marvin Gaye, including classics such as 'Ain’t No Mountain High Enough,' 'Your Precious Love,' and 'Ain’t Nothing Like the Real Thing.' Their collaborations produced multiple Top 40 hits and became enduring symbols of Motown’s soulful sound. Tragically, Terrell

item.title: UK authorities confiscate assets from former FBI fugitive James 'Whitey' Bulger
item.description: UK authorities have reportedly seized a significant amount of money from the estate or assets linked to James 'Whitey' Bulger, the notorious American gangster who was once listed among the FBI's most-wanted criminals. Bulger, who led organized crime operations in Boston, was convicted in the United States for multiple crimes, including murder, extortion, and racketeering. Following his death in 2018, authorities in both the US and UK have been investigating his financial holdings and attempting to recover assets connected to his criminal activities. The recent seizure in the UK indicates ongoing international cooperation in tracing and reclaiming funds derived from illegal activities. While the exact amount and methods of seizure have not been disclosed, legal experts emphasize that such actions are part of broader efforts to prevent criminal networks from benefiting posthumously from illicit gains. This development highlights the continuing global impact of Bulger's criminal operation

item.title: Exploring Mid-19th Century Fashion for Young Girls Through Vintage Portraits
item.description: Fashion for young girls in the mid-19th century reflected the values and aesthetics of the Victorian era, blending innocence with miniature versions of adult elegance. Portraits from this period showcase knee-length dresses with full skirts, puffed sleeves, and delicate lace trims, often made from cheerful ginghams, floral muslins, and rich velvets. A distinctive feature of the time was the pantalettes—frilly, lace-edged drawers visible beneath the hem, which combined modesty with playful style. Clothing served not only as daily wear but also as a social marker, with the length and style of a girl's dress signaling her stage of growth toward womanhood. These vintage portraits offer a detailed look into the craftsmanship, fabrics, and styles that defined childhood fashion in Victorian society, emphasizing both charm and societal expectations. Through these images, we can appreciate how fashion captured the delicate balance between youthful innocence and the early stages of societal groo

item.title: David Venturella, Trump’s new ICE director, previously acted to deport a woman linked to a presidential associate
item.description: The Trump administration has appointed David Venturella as the new head of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Venturella has a long career as an ICE agent and previously worked as an executive at the GEO Group, a private company that manages some ICE detention facilities. According to the New York Times, Venturella once intervened in the deportation of Amanda Ungaro, the mother of Paolo Zampolli's child, after she was arrested in Miami on fraud charges. Zampolli, known for introducing Donald Trump to Melania Trump, reportedly contacted Venturella to ensure her detention before she could post bail. Venturella allegedly framed the intervention as 'a favor for a friend of the president.' Ungaro remained in ICE custody for three and a half months before being deported to Brazil. Zampolli has denied using immigration enforcement to influence a custody dispute. The Times also reports that Venturella prefers discreet enforcement operations rather than highly publicized or volatile one

item.title: Speculative Scenario Suggests Trump Could Be Removed Under 25th Amendment
item.description: In a recent opinion piece, Robert Reich shared a conversation with political insiders who speculated about a potential scenario in which former President Donald Trump could be removed from office through the 25th Amendment. According to these insiders, economic challenges caused by a failed war, including rising gas and food prices, could erode support among Trump’s Republican base. In this hypothetical scenario, political figures such as JD Vance and Marco Rubio would collaborate to initiate a cabinet-led removal process, securing backing from congressional leaders including House Speaker Mike Johnson and Senate Majority Leader John Thune. Additional key figures, including Pete Hegseth and Todd Blanche, would be involved to ensure sufficient support for the process. The insiders estimated the odds of this scenario occurring at roughly 30%, noting that continued economic or political turmoil could increase the likelihood. The article outlines the potential political incentives for each

item.title: Weekly Overview of Nuclear and Global Energy Developments
item.description: This edition of non-corporate nuclear news provides a comprehensive update on global nuclear issues, energy trends, and geopolitical tensions. Key stories highlight the shift of India toward green energy instead of coal and the positive environmental impact of trawling bans in Scotland. The newsletter also addresses nuclear safety and policy concerns, including small modular reactors, nuclear waste management, and cybersecurity within the nuclear sector. Significant coverage is given to ongoing crises in Gaza and Israel, Iran-U.S. negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz, and the escalating global risks of nuclear weapons. Climate issues are emphasized, with rising CO2 levels and the environmental effects of plastic pollution highlighted. Cultural and historical perspectives include reflections on Fukushima, Chernobyl, and the Holocaust, alongside the impact of nuclear disasters on children and communities. Technology and energy debates explore the balance between renewable sources and n

item.title: Former GOP Analyst Warns Trump Policies May Harm Republicans Ahead of 2026 Midterms
item.description: Former GOP strategist Rick Wilson, co-founder of The Lincoln Project, has raised concerns about the Republican Party's strategy under former President Donald Trump as the 2026 midterms approach. Wilson argues that Trump's handling of the economy, compounded by the ongoing war in Iran, has weakened the GOP's appeal to voters. According to recent polling cited by Wilson, 70% of Americans anticipate a recession within the next year due to current policies, and over 62% believe the U.S. economy has worsened under Trump's leadership. Furthermore, 55% of respondents report that their personal financial situation has deteriorated. Wilson claims that the Republican Party's continued alignment with Trump is a tactical and strategic error, noting that prior tolerance for Trump's unconventional behavior was largely due to previous economic growth. Now, with rising consumer prices and eroding benefits from Trump-era tax cuts, the party faces a challenging electoral landscape. Wilson's analysis sug

item.title: Senator Questions Defense Secretary Hegseth on Military Priorities Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: During a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing, Senator Chris Coons of Delaware directly challenged Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, expressing concern that Hegseth was prioritizing culture war issues over military effectiveness. Coons highlighted that the ongoing conflict with Iran, initiated under President Donald Trump, had created tensions with U.S. allies. He criticized Hegseth for showing more interest in controversial topics such as book banning, the removal of diversity, equity, and inclusion content from websites, and anti-vaccine positions than in strategic military leadership. The senator also addressed recent high-profile personnel changes, including the dismissal of the Army's long-serving chief of staff and the Navy Secretary during critical military operations, suggesting these actions could negatively affect morale and operational focus. Additionally, Coons condemned Trump's focus on a White House ballroom renovation and questioned the utility of proposed large battles

item.title: Behind the Scenes with David Bowie on the Set of 'The Man Who Fell to Earth', 1975
item.description: In 1975, iconic photographer Terry O’Neill captured David Bowie in Los Angeles during the filming of Nicolas Roeg’s sci-fi classic, 'The Man Who Fell to Earth.' The photographs reveal Bowie in a detached and elegant persona, often wearing hats, sunglasses, and a suit jacket, perfectly reflecting his alien character, Thomas Jerome Newton. This period coincided with a tumultuous chapter in Bowie’s life, marked by his 'Thin White Duke' persona and heavy cocaine use while recording the album 'Station to Station.' Despite the majority of filming taking place in New Mexico’s deserts and White Sands, these striking images were taken during breaks and downtime in Los Angeles. They offer a rare glimpse into Bowie’s life off-set, capturing the style, attitude, and intensity of one of music and film’s most enigmatic figures. These photographs remain a significant record of both Bowie’s artistic evolution and the visual history of 1970s cinema.

item.title: Pima County Board Refers Perjury Allegations Against Sheriff Chris Nanos to AG After Failed Removal Attempt
item.description: A motion to remove Pima County Sheriff Chris Nanos from office failed during a recent Board of Supervisors meeting, though the board voted unanimously to refer allegations of perjury to the state attorney general. The controversy centers on claims that Nanos misrepresented his disciplinary history from his time as a Texas police officer in the 1970s and 1980s. Nanos stated under oath that he was never suspended, but records show multiple suspensions and a resignation to avoid termination. The failed removal motion was introduced by Republican Supervisor Steve Christy, but it did not receive a second, causing it to fail. Democrat Rex Scott then moved to forward the perjury allegations to the attorney general, which was approved 4-0. Critics, including Supervisors Christy and Matt Heinz, have called Nanos a public safety risk and questioned his leadership, citing a unanimous vote of no confidence from the Pima County Deputy's Organization. Nanos defended himself, saying his testimony ref

item.title: A Look at Leila Hyams’ Career and Vintage Postcards from the 1920s–1930s
item.description: Leila Hyams (1905–1977) was a celebrated American actress and model who gained fame during the 1920s and 1930s, bridging the era from silent films to early talkies. Born into a show-business family in New York City, she appeared on stage from infancy and later became a model for clothing, cosmetics, and advertisements. Hyams debuted in silent films in 1924 with Sandra and appeared in notable productions like Dancing Mothers (1926) with Clara Bow. As sound films emerged, she became a leading lady, starring in MGM’s first talkie, Alias Jimmy Valentine (1928), and films such as Spite Marriage (1929) and The Thirteenth Chair (1929). Renowned for her blonde hair, blue eyes, and wholesome beauty, she was both photogenic and stylish, appearing in glamorous gowns and simple attire. Her acting range covered light comedy, drama, and more intense roles in crime melodramas and horror films. Over a 12-year career, she appeared in more than 50 films. Hyams married Hollywood talent agent Phil Berg in

item.title: Epstein Had Dinners with British Royals, Biographer Suggests Possible Broader Connections
item.description: A biographer of the British royal family, Andrew Lownie, has revealed that Jeffrey Epstein's connections with the royals may have been broader than previously known. According to Lownie, Sophie, Duchess of Edinburgh, dined with Epstein in the mid-1990s. He suggests that her association could have lent Epstein credibility he otherwise lacked. Lownie explained that Epstein sought access to various influential individuals, including multiple members of royal families, and used these connections to bolster his reputation and gain leverage. For example, his association with Prince Andrew allowed Epstein to participate in high-profile events such as visiting Buckingham Palace and Sandringham. The biographer emphasized that the scandal may extend beyond just Prince Andrew, hinting at potential involvement or interactions with other royals. Lownie's findings were discussed on the Daily Beast's 'The Royalist Podcast.' The revelations add to the ongoing scrutiny of Epstein's social network and h

item.title: Former Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang Charged with Acting as Illegal Agent for China
item.description: Eileen Wang, a former mayor of Arcadia, California, has been charged with acting as an illegal agent of the Chinese government. According to unsealed federal documents, from late 2020 to at least 2022, Wang collaborated with her former fiancé, Yaoning 'Mike' Sun, running a website called U.S. News Center that targeted Chinese American audiences. Prosecutors allege that Wang and Sun executed instructions from Chinese officials, posting requested content and reporting back with engagement metrics. Specific incidents include posting a response to an editorial supporting a boycott of Xinjiang cotton, following guidance from the Chinese consul general in Los Angeles. Wang resigned from the Arcadia City Council after reaching a deal with federal authorities, and a magistrate judge has set a $25,000 bond, surrendered her passport, and barred her from communicating with Chinese officials. Sun, previously charged with conspiracy and acting as an illegal agent, was sentenced to four years in pri

item.title: Highlights from the LensCulture New York 2026 Photography Exhibition
item.description: LensCulture's New York 2026 Exhibition showcased an impressive collection of photographic works from 35 award-winning photographers representing 18 countries. Held concurrently with The Photography Show by AIPAD, the exhibition drew hundreds of photography enthusiasts, collectors, media members, and art lovers. Attendees had the opportunity to view the diverse and exceptional works, meet several of the photographers in person, and engage with the global photography community. The exhibition was supported by PICTO NY, which provided high-quality prints, installations, and excellent customer service. Caelum Gallery hosted the event, while Mia Allen documented both the exhibition and the opening reception. The show not only celebrated international photographic talent but also fostered personal connections among attendees, photographers, and the broader artistic community. Photos from the opening reception were captured by Peyton Fulford, highlighting the vibrant energy and engagement thr

item.title: Biographer Claims Trump May Fire Secretary of State Marco Rubio Due to Perceived Competence
item.description: According to journalist and Trump biographer Michael Wolff, President Donald Trump may consider firing Secretary of State Marco Rubio because Rubio has occasionally outshined the president, a trait Wolff describes as a 'fatal' flaw in Trump’s administration. Wolff highlighted that in Trump’s leadership style, appearing more competent or authoritative than the president can lead to professional peril. Rubio has been noted as one of the few cabinet members consistently performing his duties and attending to his responsibilities, which could inadvertently put him at odds with Trump. Wolff made these remarks during a recent episode of 'Inside Trump's Head,' co-hosted with Hugh Dougherty of The Daily Beast. The comments come shortly after Trump publicly mentioned the possibility of a Republican ticket featuring JD Vance and Marco Rubio for the 2028 election, which received lukewarm applause.
