Several other cabinet members, including Marty Makary, Kristi Noem, and Pam Bondi, have already left

item.title: Denver Airport Incident: Man Fatally Struck by Plane During Runway Trespass
item.description: A 41-year-old man, Michael Mott, died after walking onto a Denver International Airport runway and being struck by a Frontier Airlines plane headed to Los Angeles. The collision caused the plane's engine to catch fire and led to an emergency evacuation, injuring twelve passengers. Authorities have ruled Mott's death a suicide, though no note was found. Surveillance footage showed Mott scaling an eight-foot barbed wire fence and crossing the runway within minutes. Despite triggering a security alarm, the airport operator initially misinterpreted the alert as wildlife activity. Denver Airport CEO Phillip Washington emphasized that the airport’s 36-mile perimeter fence complies with FAA standards and multiple security measures are in place, including patrols, cameras, and radar systems. Police continue investigating Mott’s motivations, contacting family and acquaintances for insight. Mott had a long criminal history in Colorado, including arrests for violent felonies, domestic violence, a

item.title: Kazuko Higa and the Japanese Holdouts on Anatahan Island
item.description: During World War II, the small volcanic island of Anatahan in the Northern Mariana Islands became the stage for one of the war’s most unusual survival stories. Kazuko Higa, an Okinawan woman in her late twenties, found herself stranded with 31 Japanese men after U.S. forces bombed nearby ships in 1944. Originally married to Shoichi Higa, who left the island and never returned, Kazuko later became involved with Kikuichiro Higa, a plantation overseer. After Japan’s surrender in 1945, many of the Japanese survivors refused to believe the war had ended, hiding in the jungle and continuing to live under extreme isolation. Life on the island was tense and dangerous, with competition over Kazuko contributing to multiple deaths among the men. Kazuko reportedly navigated these threats by forming strategic alliances and shifting relationships. In 1950, she escaped into the jungle for over a month before being rescued by an American ship. The remaining holdouts surrendered the following year. Kaz

item.title: A Retrospective on Eve Arden’s Career and Portraits in the 1940s
item.description: Eve Arden, born Eunice Mary Quedens in 1908, was a celebrated American actress known for her sharp wit, comedic timing, and distinctive deadpan delivery. Throughout the 1940s, she became a reliable character actress in films, often portraying wisecracking best friends, independent women, or scene-stealing sidekicks. Arden appeared in numerous comedies, musicals, and occasional dramas, frequently taking on two to seven projects per year during the early part of the decade. Her Broadway work, including the 1941 production of 'Let’s Face It!' with Danny Kaye, showcased her talent for comedy, which she later translated into radio with 'The Danny Kaye Show' in 1945–1946. By 1948, Arden originated the role of Connie Brooks in the radio sitcom 'Our Miss Brooks,' a character that became her signature—a sardonic, single high-school English teacher with sharp humor and ongoing romantic entanglements. The show was highly popular, and Arden earned recognition as a top radio comedienne. In addition

item.title: Far-right pastor targets Vivek Ramaswamy with racist remarks during political dispute
item.description: Far-right Christian nationalist pastor Joel Webbon recently delivered a highly offensive and racist speech targeting Ohio Republican gubernatorial candidate Vivek Ramaswamy. In a video shared by Right Wing Watch, Webbon claimed that Ramaswamy, the son of Indian immigrants, should be 'demolished' to punish the GOP for nominating non-Christian candidates. He repeatedly attacked Ramaswamy's heritage, religion, and family background, making false claims about his upbringing and accusing him of being un-American. Ramaswamy, born in Cincinnati, is a businessman who previously ran for president and briefly co-led the Department of Government Efficiency with Elon Musk after the 2024 election, stepping down to focus on his gubernatorial campaign. Webbon's remarks highlight ongoing tensions within the GOP over Ramaswamy's candidacy and reflect extreme nationalist and religious rhetoric. Despite Webbon's claims, Ramaswamy’s family legally immigrated to the U.S., and he has no history of anti-Amer

item.title: Senator Questions FBI Director Kash Patel's Leadership and Conduct
item.description: During a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing, Senator Chris Van Hollen (D-MD) criticized FBI Director Kash Patel over reports questioning his personal conduct and professional decisions. Van Hollen cited allegations of excessive alcohol use, including an incident where Patel was reportedly too intoxicated for staff to gain entry into his home. Patel denied these claims. Beyond personal behavior, Van Hollen highlighted concerns about Patel's leadership, accusing him of politically motivated firings of career agents who were merely following legal procedures, as well as dismantling a counterintelligence unit tasked with monitoring Iran-related threats. The senator also alleged that Patel misused FBI resources to retaliate against former Director James Comey and to investigate journalists critical of his leadership. The hearing brought scrutiny to the FBI’s internal operations and the potential politicization of the bureau under Patel's direction. These issues raise broader questions

item.title: Analysts Highlight Messaging Struggles Affecting Trump's Political Influence
item.description: Political analysts, including Vanity Fair's Molly Jong-Fast, have observed that former President Donald Trump is struggling with an essential part of his political strategy: clear and effective messaging. During an appearance on MSNBC's 'The Beat' with Ari Melber, Jong-Fast noted that while Trump previously could stay on message even during difficult periods, he now frequently makes remarks that seem unrelated or trivial, such as discussing ballrooms or the Washington Monument's fountain. This change in communication style is concerning to Republicans who rely on disciplined messaging to maintain support among their base. In contrast, past campaigns allowed Trump to focus on specific promises and policy points, even if outcomes did not match expectations. Analysts argue that this shift in focus and the lack of coherent messaging could affect the Republican Party's performance in upcoming elections, mirroring losses seen in previous midterms. Jong-Fast emphasized that the disjointed app

item.title: Researchers Identify Molecular Mechanism Linking Brown Fat Activation to Bone Health
item.description: Researchers at McGill University have identified a previously unknown molecular mechanism that activates an alternative energy-burning pathway in brown fat, potentially opening new avenues for treatments targeting bone health. Brown fat, unlike white fat, burns calories to generate heat. The study reveals that glycerol, a molecule released when fat is metabolized in response to cold, binds to an enzyme called TNAP in a specific 'glycerol pocket,' triggering the futile creatine cycle—a secondary pathway for heat production. Beyond its role in metabolism, TNAP is essential for bone mineralization, and mutations reducing its activity can cause hypophosphatasia, a rare disorder characterized by weak bones and skeletal abnormalities. By studying TNAP mutations, the team found that this fat-burning switch also directly influences bone-forming cells, suggesting potential therapeutic strategies to enhance bone strength. The research, published in Nature, involved collaboration with multiple in

item.title: Trump Shrugs Off Economic Concerns While Discussing Iran
item.description: During a press interaction outside the White House, President Donald Trump surprised CNN anchors by openly dismissing the economic hardships Americans are facing due to his ongoing conflict with Iran. When asked about the economic impact of the war, Trump stated that his focus is on preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons and that he does not consider the financial struggles of American citizens. This statement came amid reports showing a 3.8% increase in inflation over the past year, largely driven by energy price hikes linked to the conflict. CNN hosts Erin Burnett and Kristen Holmes expressed disbelief, highlighting that White House officials have consistently warned Trump about the political and economic risks associated with rising inflation and higher gas prices. Analysts noted that Trump's previous success in the 2024 election was partly due to his team's attention to economic concerns that mattered to voters. By downplaying these issues, Trump appears to be distancing hi

item.title: The Life and Art of Savely Sorin, Portraitist of the Elite
item.description: Savely Sorin (1878–1953) was a distinguished portrait painter whose work merged the formal traditions of the Russian Academy with the sophisticated cosmopolitan style of the early 20th century. Born in Russia, Sorin achieved international recognition with successful stints in Paris and New York, becoming a favored artist for royalty, celebrities, and high-society figures. His portraits are notable for their meticulous precision and luminous, almost ethereal palettes, giving subjects a timeless, porcelain-like elegance. Sorin’s oeuvre includes portraits of prominent figures such as Queen Consort Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon, the ballerina Anna Pavlova, and members of aristocratic and high-profile families. Beyond mere likenesses, his paintings captured the poise, refinement, and subtle grandeur of a world undergoing social and cultural transformation. Through his art, Sorin immortalized the grace and sophistication of his era, making him a trusted chronicler of aristocratic life and elite circl

item.title: A Visual History of Ladies’ Home Journal Covers from the Early 20th Century
item.description: Founded in 1883, the Ladies’ Home Journal became one of the most influential magazines in American history, guiding women’s domestic, social, and cultural lives throughout the 20th century. At its height, the magazine was celebrated for its artistic covers, compelling advice columns such as “Can This Marriage Be Saved?”, and contributions from notable writers and political figures. Beyond recipes, fashion, and home tips, the magazine tackled important social issues including women’s health, education, and suffrage, reflecting and shaping the evolving identity of American women. This collection highlights 40 covers from the early 1900s, showcasing the changing styles, societal values, and aspirations of women during that period. Each cover serves as a vivid window into the past, preserving a century-old perspective on domestic life, culture, and fashion. The collection demonstrates how visual art and print media influenced public perception and offered both guidance and inspiration to g

item.title: Flossie Rogers: A Story of Polio, Resilience, and Nursing Compassion
item.description: In June 1957, Flossie Rogers, a young woman affected by polio, experienced a small yet profound moment of dignity and hope when a nurse held up a mirror so she could see her own reflection. Confined to an iron lung and facing an uncertain future, Flossie's struggle with polio was both physical and emotional, marked by months of therapy, isolation, and dependence on others. The compassionate care provided by nurses left a lasting impression, shaping her perception of humanity and inspiring her future career. Flossie went on to become a nurse herself, dedicating approximately 35 years to the profession and focusing on patient dignity and empathetic care, reflecting the same support she once received. Her life story highlights the human aspects of medical recovery, the importance of compassionate care, and the resilience of those who survive serious illness. Flossie passed away in 2018 at the age of 84, leaving behind a legacy of both personal courage and professional dedication.

item.title: Theoretical Study on Rapid Mars Missions Highlights Energy Limits
item.description: Recent media reports suggested that a Brazilian physicist, Marcelo de Oliveira Souza, discovered an accidental shortcut to Mars that could halve travel time. However, a closer look at his study, published in Acta Astronautica, reveals a far less dramatic reality. Souza analyzed preliminary orbital data of the asteroid 2001 CA21 and used its initially estimated orbit as a geometric template to explore faster Earth-to-Mars trajectories using the classical Lambert Problem. While the study calculated a potential Mars round-trip lasting 153 days, achieving this would require an initial velocity of 32.5 km/s—double that of the fastest spacecraft ever launched, New Horizons, and far beyond current propulsion capabilities, including Starship or New Glenn. Even the more moderate 226-day mission demands an initial speed of 16.5 km/s, still pushing technological limits. The real contribution of Souza's work lies in applying orbital plane constraints from near-Earth asteroids to filter possible in

item.title: Trump DOJ claims authority to access state voter rolls despite court rulings
item.description: The Department of Justice under President Donald Trump released a memo asserting that the department has the authority to obtain state voter registration lists, contradicting multiple federal court decisions. The DOJ has sued 30 states and the District of Columbia for not providing unredacted voter data, which includes sensitive information like driver's license numbers and partial Social Security numbers. Courts in California, Michigan, and Oregon have ruled against the DOJ, stating it lacks legal authority to compel states to share such information. Despite these rulings, the DOJ maintains that obtaining voter rolls is part of its duty to protect election integrity. In January, FBI agents collected over 600 boxes of election data from Fulton County, Georgia. The memo cites the Civil Rights Act and other federal laws to justify this action, claiming that sharing data with the Department of Homeland Security is permitted for identifying ineligible voters, including undocumented immigra

item.title: Trump says U.S. economic concerns do not influence his Iran policy, drawing criticism
item.description: On May 12, 2026, President Donald Trump made a statement outside the White House regarding U.S. negotiations to end the war in Iran. Trump asserted that the financial struggles Americans face do not impact his approach, emphasizing that his focus is solely on preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons. This comment drew criticism and mockery from political commentator David Pakman, who highlighted the apparent disconnect between Trump's priorities and the economic realities faced by the public. At the time, inflation had risen to 3.8% over the past year, the highest level since the COVID-19 pandemic, primarily driven by increasing energy costs linked to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Public sentiment toward Trump's economic handling remains negative, with a CNN poll indicating that 70% of Americans disapprove of his approach. Pakman sarcastically noted that Trump's billionaire status shields him from everyday financial worries, suggesting that his lack of concern for ordinary citize

item.title: Senator Lindsey Graham Expresses Frustration Over Pakistan’s Role in Iran Conflict
item.description: During a Senate hearing on Tuesday regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran, Senator Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) expressed visible frustration with military officials and questioned Pakistan’s role as a neutral mediator. Addressing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Dan Caine, Graham challenged reports that Pakistan may have allowed Iranian aircraft to use its bases, which could undermine its position as a peace mediator. When both officials declined to provide a definitive answer due to the classified nature of negotiations, Graham sharply pressed for clarity, asserting that he wanted to be directly involved in the discussions. He stated that if Pakistan is indeed protecting Iranian military assets, the United States might need to seek alternative mediators. Despite Graham’s critical stance, President Donald Trump dismissed concerns about Pakistan, praising its leadership ahead of his trip to China. The exchange highlighted growing tensions and skepticism within the U.

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates as Ceasefire Talks Stall and Global Energy Shock Worsens
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the U.S.-Israeli coalition and Iran continues to escalate, with President Trump describing the current ceasefire as 'on life support' after Iran rejected the latest U.S. peace proposal, calling it 'totally unacceptable.' Iran maintains that it is defending its legitimate rights and demands no concessions. Meanwhile, diplomatic efforts continue, with Trump planning to urge China to use its influence over Iran during his upcoming visit to Beijing. Israel has sent Iron Dome batteries and personnel to the UAE to bolster regional defenses, while strikes in southern Lebanon have killed six people amid ongoing clashes with Hezbollah. The conflict has caused global energy disruption, with Saudi Aramco warning of the largest supply shock ever experienced, potentially lasting until 2027. Oil prices have surged above $104 per barrel, prompting U.S. measures including new sanctions on Iranian entities and plans to suspend the federal gas tax. Humanitarian concerns are

item.title: Georgia GOP Candidate Receives Death Threat Ahead of Bomb Scare at Campaign Event
item.description: Brad Raffensperger, the Republican candidate for governor of Georgia and current Secretary of State, received a serious death threat on Monday, a day before a bomb scare at one of his campaign events. The threat came in the form of a four-page handwritten manifesto, accompanied by a photo of Raffensperger with the word 'Boom' written across his forehead. Campaign officials confirmed the document targeted Raffensperger, who has faced numerous threats since the 2020 presidential election for refusing to overturn Georgia's results in favor of former President Donald Trump. The manifesto was sent to the Clay County Sheriff's Office, prompting notification of the FBI and state law enforcement, who increased security for Raffensperger and his family. The following day, Raffensperger planned to hold a campaign event in Macon, Georgia, at the Middle Georgia Regional Airport. The event was canceled after authorities discovered a suspicious object in a vending machine, although it remains unclea

item.title: Researchers Identify Statistical Chemical Patterns That May Indicate Extraterrestrial Life
item.description: Researchers at the University of California - Riverside have developed a new statistical approach to detect potential extraterrestrial life. Instead of focusing on specific molecules, the team analyzed patterns in the distribution of amino acids and fatty acids, discovering that biological systems leave behind unique organizational signatures that differ from nonliving chemistry. Using data from microbes, soils, fossils, meteorites, asteroids, and lab simulations, they found that amino acids in living systems are more diverse and evenly distributed, while fatty acids in nonliving systems show higher uniformity. This method allowed them to distinguish between biological and nonbiological samples, even when the samples were degraded, such as fossilized dinosaur eggshells. The researchers adapted metrics from ecology, like species richness and evenness, to chemical datasets, creating a novel tool for astrobiology. While this statistical framework alone cannot confirm life, it provides an

item.title: Trump DOJ Considers Settling $10 Billion IRS Lawsuit Amid Conflict of Interest Concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Justice is reportedly considering settling a $10 billion lawsuit that Trump filed against the Internal Revenue Service regarding the leak of his 2019 tax returns. The lawsuit, initiated in January, claims that the IRS failed to prevent the public disclosure of his tax information. Reports indicate that the potential settlement has sparked widespread criticism and concerns about conflicts of interest, as the IRS is an executive agency overseen by the President. Political commentators and public figures expressed outrage on social media, citing potential corruption and misuse of government resources. Some warned that such a settlement could be viewed as an attempt by Trump to leverage the Justice Department for personal gain. Analysts note that the situation raises broader questions about accountability, transparency, and the ethical responsibilities of elected officials when legal disputes intersect with governmental authority. The ongoing debate r

item.title: Xavier Becerra Requests Profile-Focused Interview Amid California Governor Campaign
item.description: Democratic California gubernatorial candidate Xavier Becerra, former U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary under the Biden administration, faced scrutiny after a tense exchange with KTLA reporter Annie Rose Ramos during an interview. Becerra emphasized that he wanted the discussion to be a 'profile piece' rather than a 'gotcha piece,' clarifying that he hoped the interview would focus on his accomplishments and plans while still addressing tough questions. Ramos responded that her definition of a profile included both accomplishments and hard questions, highlighting a minor disagreement over framing the interview. The discussion drew attention on social media, with critics describing Becerra's comments as an attempt to control the interview narrative, and some comparing the interaction to a similar exchange between rival candidate Katie Porter and CBS California last year. Despite the controversy, Becerra did answer substantive questions about addressing homelessness and his record

item.title: Foxconn Cyberattack Exposes Risks in Global Manufacturing Supply Chains
item.description: Foxconn, a major electronics manufacturer known for producing Apple iPhones, has suffered a ransomware attack that could compromise sensitive data from its customers, including Dell, Google, Apple, and Nvidia. The attackers, identified as the Nitrogen group, claim to have stolen 8 terabytes of information, including schematics and project details. While Foxconn has not confirmed the full extent of the breach, it acknowledged cyberattacks at some North American factories, which have since resumed normal operations. Nitrogen, active since 2023, targets manufacturing, technology, and retail sectors and is linked to the notorious ALPHV/BlackCat group. Previous ransomware attacks on Foxconn include incidents in Mexico in 2020 and 2022, and a 2024 attack on a subsidiary, Foxsemicon Integrated Technology. The group often uses ransomware that encrypts systems, but their software has a flaw that makes decryption impossible once files are locked. The attack highlights how companies managing crit

item.title: 1960 Encounter Between Filipino Fisherman and USS Triton During Secret Submarine Voyage
item.description: On April 1, 1960, 19-year-old Filipino fisherman Rufino Baring had an extraordinary encounter with the USS Triton (SSRN-586), a nuclear-powered submarine of the U.S. Navy. The Triton was undertaking Operation Sandblast, the first fully submerged circumnavigation of the globe, covering over 60,000 miles under the command of Captain Edward L. Beach. During the voyage, the submarine remained submerged for nearly its entire 83-day journey, demonstrating the capabilities of nuclear-powered submarines during the Cold War. While transiting the Bohol Strait, the Triton briefly raised its periscope, and Baring, paddling nearby in a small canoe, mistook it for a sea monster’s eye. The moment was captured in a photograph, likely taken by National Geographic photographer Joseph Baylor Roberts, and later featured in National Geographic. This incident marked Baring as the only civilian to visually spot the Triton during its secret mission. The encounter illustrates a striking contrast between tradit

item.title: Short-Term Diet Adjustments May Reduce Biological Age in Older Adults
item.description: A recent study from the University of Sydney suggests that short-term dietary changes can influence biological aging in adults aged 65 to 75. In a four-week trial involving 104 participants, researchers tested four different diets that varied in fat content and plant-based versus animal-based protein ratios. The study measured biological age using 20 biomarkers, including cholesterol, insulin, and C-reactive protein. Results indicated that participants who reduced fat intake or increased plant-based protein consumption showed measurable reductions in biological age, with the most significant improvement observed in those following an omnivorous diet higher in carbohydrates and lower in fat. Participants who continued a high-fat diet similar to their usual eating habits experienced little to no change. While these findings are promising, scientists caution that longer and larger studies are necessary to determine whether these short-term improvements translate into sustained health bene

item.title: James Comer avoids answering questions about Trump's statements on Americans' financial concerns
item.description: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer (R-KY) faced reporters regarding President Donald Trump's recent comments about the rising cost-of-living crisis. When asked by Meidas Touch reporter Pablo Manríquez about Trump stating that he does not consider Americans' financial situation, Comer initially denied the remark, claiming he hadn't heard it. Despite repeated clarifications from the reporter, Comer insisted that every time he speaks with Trump, the president is focused on affordability. When pressed further about gas prices, Comer declined to answer, effectively ending the conversation. The exchange highlights the tension between elected officials and the media over accountability for economic issues, especially given the public's concern about inflation and the cost of living. Comer's evasive responses reflect the challenges faced by politicians when addressing controversial statements from high-profile leaders they support.

item.title: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary Steps Down Following Agency Restructuring
item.description: Marty Makary, the Commissioner of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, has resigned after a period of internal turmoil within the agency. His departure comes after White House staff approved a plan to replace him, signaling a shift in leadership priorities. The changes at the FDA are part of a broader shake-up at the Department of Health and Human Services, where some officials appointed by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. are being replaced with individuals who have more traditional experience in regulatory and health roles. Kyle Diamantas, who recently oversaw the FDA’s food program and was promoted to a senior counselor role under Secretary Kennedy, has been named the acting leader of the agency. President Donald Trump announced Diamantas’s temporary appointment on his Truth Social platform. The resignation and leadership changes highlight ongoing tensions and differing approaches within the federal health administration, as officials seek to balance innovation with regulato

item.title: Rep. Virginia Foxx responds to fourth grader’s question on electric vehicle rebates with concerns over national debt
item.description: U.S. Representative Virginia Foxx (R-NC), a member of the MAGA wing of the Republican Party, recently responded to a letter from a 10-year-old student asking about a $5,000 tax rebate for purchasing electric vehicles. Foxx, 82, wrote back emphasizing the national debt as a more pressing issue, stating that such tax rebates would take 'money out of the pockets of hardworking people.' She also included an article explaining the urgency of addressing the national debt and suggested that the child ask their teacher about propaganda. The response drew widespread attention and criticism on social media, with parents, journalists, and political observers describing it as inappropriate and condescending for a young constituent. Critics highlighted that the response could discourage civic engagement among children and portrayed Foxx as out of touch with her constituents. The incident sparked debate over how elected officials communicate with younger citizens and whether public figures should pr

item.title: Trump Administration Promotes Fertility Policies Amid Criticism Over Approach
item.description: The Trump administration, through initiatives like Moms.gov and recent maternal health events, has emphasized policies aimed at increasing fertility rates in the United States. These efforts include encouraging employers to offer IVF and other fertility treatments, though participation is optional. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. highlighted declining sperm counts among teenagers, calling it a national crisis, despite studies showing no significant decline since 1970. Mehmet Oz described a growing number of Americans as 'underbabied,' citing this trend as a potential economic concern. Experts note that factors such as high housing and healthcare costs, lack of universal childcare, and limited paid family leave play a larger role in declining birth rates than fertility treatments alone. Critics argue that these policies reflect a pronatalist agenda that prioritizes control over women’s reproductive choices rather than addressing structural obstacles to family f

item.title: Muhammad Ali Spars Gently with Young Fan During 1963 London Training
item.description: On June 6, 1963, in a small London gym, six-year-old Patrick Power was taking boxing lessons to defend himself against schoolyard bullies. That day became unforgettable when Muhammad Ali, then known as Cassius Clay, arrived to prepare for his upcoming fight against British champion Henry Cooper. Patrick had no idea he would share the ring, even briefly, with the future legend. Ali, renowned for both his talent and charm, noticed Patrick and offered him some boxing advice. In a display of kindness and playful generosity, Ali sparred gently with the young boy, allowing him to land a few punches and 'win' the round. Photographs from that day capture Ali crouching with a wide grin while Patrick throws a punch with determination. This rare glimpse of Ali’s warmth outside the public eye transformed an ordinary boxing lesson into a magical memory for the child and those present, highlighting the human side of one of the world’s greatest athletes.

item.title: 1975 Chicago Auto Show Highlights and Style Icons
item.description: The 1975 Chicago Auto Show, held at McCormick Place from February 22 to March 2, represented a significant moment in American automotive history. In the wake of the energy crisis, the industry began focusing on fuel efficiency, signaling a shift in priorities for U.S. manufacturers. The event was notable not only for its display of the newest cars but also for the models, known as “Auto Show Girls,” who added glamour and personality to the exhibition. These women were dressed in styles ranging from elegant evening wear to casual 1970s leisure suits, reflecting the fashion trends of the era. Their presence brought a human touch to the large exhibition halls filled with luxury sedans and bold new coupes. The show captured the essence of mid-70s culture, highlighting both the excitement and challenges faced by the American automotive industry. Attendees and photographers alike celebrated the combination of style, innovation, and cultural expression on display at this iconic event.

item.title: A Glimpse into Outdoor Life in 1850s and 1860s America Through Rare Photographs
item.description: The mid-19th century in America was a period of exploration, survival, and transformation. This collection of rare vintage photographs captures everyday life and outdoor experiences during the 1850s and 1860s, revealing a world where the wilderness and human resilience intersected. Images show Civil War camps, family life, and communities gathering in towns and natural settings, highlighting both the hardships and simple joys of the era. Children ride horses, men work with tools, and families pose in front of homes, reflecting the social and cultural norms of the time. Scenic photographs showcase locations such as Lookout Mountain in Tennessee, Minnehaha Falls in Minnesota, and Saratoga Springs in New York, preserving both natural landscapes and architectural elements. The collection also includes moments of leisure, military presence, and everyday labor, providing a comprehensive view of life before industrialization reshaped American society. These photographs not only record faces a

item.title: Ex-GOP Lawmaker Questions Trump Mobile's Gold Phone Claims and U.S. Manufacturing
item.description: Former Republican Congressman Adam Kinzinger expressed disbelief over the Trump family's new gold smartphone, the T1, marketed under Trump Mobile. Initially advertised as 'Made in USA' and priced at $499, the device's production is reportedly overseas, with only minimal assembly in Miami. Despite $59 million in deposits from approximately 590,000 buyers, no confirmed customer has received a phone. Kinzinger highlighted that the fine print now states the deposit does not guarantee production or delivery, raising concerns about potential misleading marketing. Analysts suggest the T1 is a reskinned version of the Wingtech Revvl 7 Pro 5G, manufactured in China, sold at about a third of the Trump phone's price. The 'Made in USA' wording has been quietly replaced with ambiguous language like 'designed with American values.' Democratic senators, including Elizabeth Warren, have urged the FTC to investigate possible false advertising and bait-and-switch tactics. Trump Mobile maintains that dep

item.title: ICE awarded $12 million contract to company with fabricated staff and misleading claims
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) awarded a $12.2 million no-bid contract to Edge Ops LLC, a company whose background appears highly questionable, according to investigative reporting by The Lever. Edge Ops, initially founded in 2014 as a legal entity to hold its founder's sailboat, pivoted to defense and surveillance contracting after Donald Trump's 2024 election victory. The company's Project SAFE HAVEN is an AI-based surveillance tool intended to track immigrants' movements, routines, and categorize them as potential threats. However, The Lever discovered multiple fabrications on the company’s website. The so-called lead computer scientist was a stock photo, while client testimonials and claims about contributions to wildfire detection technology were either false or misattributed. When contacted, the original technology companies cited had no knowledge of working with Edge Ops. After the reporting, Edge Ops overhauled its website, removing mentions of Project SAFE HAVE

item.title: Christy Lee Rogers Creates Underwater Portraits Evoking Baroque Art
item.description: Photographer Christy Lee Rogers has gained attention for her remarkable underwater photography that resembles classical Baroque paintings. By submerging models in flowing fabrics during nighttime shoots, Rogers skillfully manipulates light and movement to achieve dramatic, oil-paint-like visuals reminiscent of Caravaggio’s work. The photographs capture the fluidity of fabrics and the graceful interactions of the models, creating a serene and ethereal atmosphere. Beyond the artistic achievement, the technical execution is impressive, requiring models to hold their breath and move carefully underwater to compose each shot. The images aim to represent a peaceful, imaginary place where viewers can experience beauty and freedom. A short behind-the-scenes video accompanies the collection, offering insight into the complex process. Rogers’ work has been shared widely on platforms like Facebook, Instagram, Pinterest, and YouTube, highlighting her innovative approach to blending photography and

item.title: Indy 500 Qualifying Format Adjusted for 2026 Race
item.description: The Indianapolis 500 is making minor changes to its qualifying procedure for the 110th Running this year. Traditionally, the Indy 500 uses a unique qualifying format where drivers complete four-lap runs, and the field is set based on the average speed of those laps. After an initial run, teams can choose to queue for additional attempts or risk discarding previous times for a chance to improve their positions, creating high-stakes tension, particularly in the final minutes. This year, with exactly 33 entries and no bump day, organizers have adjusted the process slightly. The fastest nine drivers from the first day of qualifying will secure spots in the Top 12, while drivers ranked 10th through 15th will compete for the remaining three positions. Following the determination of the Top 12, qualifying will continue in the usual manner. These tweaks aim to maintain excitement despite a full field of entries and the absence of traditional bumping drama. Practice began on May 12, with qualif

item.title: Iran Deploys Small Naval Vessels to Disrupt Shipping in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Following the near-total destruction of Iran's conventional naval fleet by U.S. and Israeli strikes, Tehran has adopted an asymmetrical maritime strategy focused on small, fast-attack vessels known as the 'mosquito fleet.' Operated by the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC), these vessels are equipped with missiles, machine guns, and drones, and are designed to swarm and disrupt commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts note that Iran’s advantage lies in the sheer number of boats—over a thousand—and their integration with drones, mines, and hidden coastal infrastructure, which collectively create a persistent threat that is difficult to neutralize. This strategy prioritizes disruption and attrition rather than seeking decisive naval battles, allowing Iran to exert coercive leverage without escalating into full-scale war. Recent incidents, including the seizure of container ships, demonstrate the fleet’s effectiveness. Experts emphasize that eliminating these small boa

item.title: WSJ Criticizes Trump's Proposal to Make Venezuela the 51st U.S. State
item.description: The Wall Street Journal editorial board criticized President Donald Trump's latest proposal to make Venezuela the 51st state of the United States. Trump, who previously expressed interest in Greenland and Canada, suggested during a Fox News interview that he is seriously considering annexing Venezuela. However, Venezuela remains a dictatorship under Delcy Rodríguez, who continues the policies of former leader Nicolás Maduro. Despite this, Trump has praised Rodríguez for restoring oil production in the country, drawing criticism for appearing unconcerned about democratic processes or human rights. Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado remains under threat of arrest and possible assassination, highlighting the risks for those opposing the regime. The editorial board warned that while Trump claims Venezuelans support him, the populace may not appreciate his alignment with Maduro 2.0. This development coincides with ongoing tensions in Iran, where Trump's policies have disrupte

item.title: FDA Commissioner Dr. Marty Makary Steps Down Amid Dispute Over Flavored Vapes
item.description: Dr. Marty Makary, the Commissioner of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), resigned from his position on May 12, 2026, after ongoing disagreements with President Donald Trump. The conflict largely revolved around the approval of flavored vape nicotine products. The Trump administration had been pushing to expand access to these flavored vapes, while Dr. Makary's office issued a memo blocking several flavors from retail authorization. This disagreement reportedly put him 'on thin ice' with the President, and anonymous sources suggest that the decision to remove him had been planned for several months. Dr. Makary's tenure at the FDA was marked by significant internal turmoil, including mass layoffs, senior leadership turnover, and policy disputes with lawmakers and pharmaceutical companies. His approach to regulatory decisions and agency management often clashed with the Trump administration's priorities, creating a contentious atmosphere within the agency. This resignation marks

item.title: A Look Back at Jackie Magazine Covers and 1970s Teen Fashion
item.description: Jackie magazine was one of the most influential British teen publications of the 20th century, launched on January 11, 1964, by D.C. Thomson & Co. It gained immense popularity during the 1970s, becoming the best-selling girls' magazine in the UK. Aimed at teenage girls, Jackie offered a mix of fashion and beauty tips, pop culture posters, photo love stories, and advice columns famously answered by Cathy and Claire. The magazine also featured lighthearted articles about friendship, boys, and growing up, shaping the dreams, tastes, and insecurities of generations of British girls. At its peak, Jackie sold over 600,000 copies weekly, reflecting the cultural and social trends of the time. Its relatively wholesome and innocent tone distinguished it from later publications, and it remained a cultural touchstone until its final issue in July 1993. The vibrant magazine covers from the 1970s preserved in this article showcase the era's fashion, youthful optimism, and distinctive teenage culture

item.title: Michigan GOP Senate Candidate Faces Scrutiny Over Campaign Ties to Church With Convicted Leaders
item.description: Mike Rogers, a Republican candidate for the U.S. Senate seat in Michigan, is under scrutiny for his campaign's association with Living Word Church, whose leadership has faced serious child sex abuse convictions. Rogers is competing to replace Democrat Gary Peters, while the Democratic primary features Rep. Haley Stevens, state Sen. Mallory McMorrow, and former Detroit health director Abdul El-Sayed. Controversy arose after two church leaders involved with Rogers’ volunteer coalition were linked to criminal activity at Living Word Church, where three leaders have been convicted of sexual offenses against children. Tim Cross, a coalition leader, resigned from the campaign without directly referencing the scandal, while another leader, Brian Ford, remains involved despite supporting convicted former youth leader James Randolph, who received a 25-40 year prison sentence. Critics, including former church members Dana and Dan Stahl, have publicly challenged Rogers for remaining silent on the

item.title: Republicans Investigate Sam Altman’s Investments Amid Questions About Trump Family Finances
item.description: Republican lawmakers are investigating whether OpenAI CEO Sam Altman violated conflict-of-interest laws through his personal investments, while reportedly overlooking financial dealings of the Trump family. The House Oversight Committee has sent Altman a letter inquiring whether funds donated to OpenAI's nonprofit branch were used for unintended purposes. They are also examining whether Altman's investments in companies like Helion, a nuclear fusion startup, could have increased OpenAI's value ahead of a potential IPO. Several Republican state attorneys general have requested the SEC to investigate Altman for possible 'self-dealing.' This follows similar claims made by Elon Musk in a lawsuit concerning OpenAI's for-profit conversion. Altman has previously supported Republican-backed AI regulations and donated $1 million to Trump’s inauguration fund. Critics note that while Altman faces scrutiny, the Trump family has engaged in foreign investments and deals that have not been similarly

item.title: White House Seeks Congressional Support for Trump's Ballroom Project Amid Republican Opposition
item.description: The White House is actively lobbying Republican lawmakers in Congress to secure $1 billion in funding for security measures related to President Donald Trump's proposed ballroom project, according to Politico. Secret Service Director Sean Curran met with Senate Republicans to explain that only a portion of the requested funds would go toward the ballroom itself, but the reception was largely skeptical. Administration officials plan to continue private meetings throughout the week to stress the necessity of funding security for the ballroom and other venues frequently used by the president. However, key Republicans, including Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick of Pennsylvania, have firmly opposed any federal money being allocated for the project. Trump had previously promised that the ballroom, which has already involved the demolition of the East Wing of the White House, would be funded entirely by private corporate donations rather than taxpayer dollars. The ongoing debate highlights internal GOP

item.title: Miles Robinson Eyes First World Cup Appearance with USMNT After Injury Comeback
item.description: Miles Robinson, a key center-back for the United States men's national soccer team (USMNT), is eagerly anticipating the possibility of being selected for his first FIFA World Cup in 2026. Robinson, who missed the 2022 World Cup due to a ruptured Achilles just one month before the tournament, describes this opportunity as a 'full-circle' moment that would mean a great deal to him. Over the past four years, he has focused on improving both mentally and physically, overcoming the challenges that came with his injury. Robinson emphasizes the importance of self-belief and gratitude for the support of friends, family, and teammates during difficult times. Currently, he is concentrating on his club, FC Cincinnati, preparing for key matches in the Major League Soccer season, including a rematch against Inter Miami. While awaiting the roster announcement on May 26, Robinson is thankful for the chance to potentially represent the U.S. on soccer's biggest stage. Off the field, he enjoys grilling

item.title: Major tech and semiconductor stocks slip after mixed earnings and profit-taking
item.description: Technology and semiconductor stocks fell sharply after recent earnings reports and profit-taking left several large names markedly lower. Chipmakers including Micron and Intel saw double-digit declines, while Qualcomm dropped even more steeply as investors digested results and paused after a rapid rally in AI-related and semiconductor shares. Market participants described the move as a typical post-earnings pullback: many of the affected stocks had run up heavily in recent weeks, so the earnings window provided a chance for traders to lock in gains. Broader market reaction was influenced by the latest inflation data and macroeconomic headlines, which increased uncertainty about the timing of interest-rate cuts and reduced risk appetite for richly valued sectors. Analysts noted that while the near-term price action was painful, fundamentals for several companies remain intact, and some pullbacks may present buying opportunities for longer-term investors. Overall, the selloff appeared co

item.title: Bianca Jagger’s Elegant and Iconic Fashion in the 1980s
item.description: Bianca Jagger’s fashion in the 1980s evolved from her iconic 1970s glamour into a more polished and sophisticated European style. Known for her socialite presence in New York and Europe, she frequently attended galas, parties, and high-profile events, blending her love for drama, tailoring, and versatility. Her wardrobe featured sharp tuxedo jackets with satin lapels, structured suits, and double-breasted blazers, often paired with sheer tops or bodysuits. White suits and wide-leg trousers remained signature elements, echoing her famous YSL wedding look but updated for the 1980s. She also embraced dramatic eveningwear, including strapless gowns, off-the-shoulder dresses, bustier or lace dresses, and hoop-skirted ballgowns. Accessories were a key part of her style, with opulent hats, chokers, high heels, and occasional bold touches like canes or furs. Monochrome palettes, especially black and white, and contrasts between masculine-tailored elements and feminine draping defined her aesth

item.title: Apolipoprotein B Offers a More Accurate Measure of Cholesterol-Related Heart Risk
item.description: For decades, LDL cholesterol, often referred to as 'bad cholesterol,' has been the primary measure for assessing cardiovascular risk, guiding both clinical guidelines and widespread statin use. While lowering LDL has proven benefits in reducing heart attacks, strokes, and early death, it does not provide a complete picture of risk. LDL cholesterol tests measure the amount of cholesterol within particles, but not the number of particles themselves, which is a key factor in plaque buildup and artery blockage. Apolipoprotein B (apoB) represents the total number of cholesterol-carrying particles and has emerged as a more precise predictor of cardiovascular events. Despite evidence supporting its accuracy and cost-effectiveness, apoB testing is not widely adopted due to decades of reliance on LDL cholesterol, simplicity of the test, and established treatment protocols. Experts emphasize that apoB alone does not indicate the underlying causes of elevated risk, which could range from high LDL

item.title: Ex-GOP strategist criticizes Trump for strengthening China's position ahead of visit
item.description: Ahead of President Donald Trump's visit to China from May 12-15, ex-GOP strategist Rick Wilson criticized Trump for actions he believes have inadvertently advanced China's strategic and economic ambitions. Wilson highlighted that Trump's low approval ratings and perceived political weakness, coupled with ongoing global issues such as the war in Iran, undermine the U.S. position internationally. He specifically pointed to Trump's trade policies, which have alienated nations in South America, leading them to strengthen trade ties with China. Additionally, Wilson criticized Trump's decision to dismantle USAID programs, arguing that it has allowed China to step in as a major provider of aid in Africa, further boosting China's global influence. The strategist warned that Trump's visit will likely showcase a contrast in negotiating power, with Xi Jinping holding the stronger position. The discussion frames Trump's recent policies as inadvertently empowering China on multiple fronts, includin

item.title: LAPD recovers $500,000 in stolen merchandise after adult toy company uncovers illegal operation
item.description: The Los Angeles Police Department recently recovered over $500,000 in stolen goods thanks to the efforts of a European adult toy company. The company discovered that its stolen merchandise was being sold online and purchased it, then provided the shipping information to authorities. This led the LAPD’s Cargo Theft Task Force to a used car lot where a raid uncovered not only adult products but also power tools, acoustic drum parts, boxes of Nike jeans, Yeti mugs, and Acer computers. Peter Ahoubim of Beverly Hills was arrested on suspicion of receiving stolen property. The case highlights Los Angeles’ vulnerability to cargo theft, including both train and commercial shipments. Authorities are warning consumers to be cautious when buying online, especially when return policies are absent and prices seem unusually low, as these could indicate stolen merchandise. The adult toy company’s initiative played a key role in disrupting this illegal operation, demonstrating how private businesses c

item.title: Kevin Hartz’s Venture Firm A* Raises $450 Million for Third Fund
item.description: Early-stage venture capital firm A*, led by serial entrepreneur Kevin Hartz and Bennet Siegel, announced the successful closing of its third fund totaling $450 million. Founded in 2020, A* takes a generalist investment approach, supporting startups across multiple sectors such as artificial intelligence, fintech, healthcare, and security. The fund plans to make investments ranging from $3 million to $5 million per company, targeting at least 30 startups over the next two to three years, consistent with the deployment strategy of its previous funds. Notable limited partners include nonprofits, foundations, endowments, and Carnegie Mellon University. A* has a history of supporting unusually young founders, with around 20% of its portfolio consisting of teenage entrepreneurs. Among its investments are fintech company Ramp and AI firm Mercor. This latest fund follows Fund II, which raised $315 million in 2024, and Fund I, which raised $300 million in 2021. Kevin Hartz is recognized for co-

item.title: A Visual History of Berlin in 1966: Life on Both Sides of the Wall
item.description: In 1966, Berlin was a city marked by stark contrasts and historical significance. The Berlin Wall physically and ideologically divided the city into East and West, yet daily life in both sectors exhibited distinct cultural and social characteristics. West Berlin’s streets, such as Kurfürstendamm and Bismarckstraße, reflected post-war prosperity and modernization, with bustling shops, cinemas, and contemporary vehicles like Mercedes-Benz. Fashionable pedestrians and the city’s commercial energy highlighted the ongoing 'Economic Miracle.' Meanwhile, East Berlin presented a more somber, organized atmosphere, showcasing monumental socialist architecture along Unter den Linden, Karl-Marx-Allee, and landmarks like the State Opera, Humboldt University, and the Brandenburg Gate. Construction projects, including the TV Tower, symbolized progress amidst a controlled environment. Vintage photographs from this period offer an intimate glimpse into the city’s dual reality—one side embracing capital

item.title: Trump’s Iran War Struggles and Its Potential Impact on US-Israel Relations
item.description: The article analyzes the consequences of the United States’ ongoing military conflict with Iran under former President Trump, which has now lasted 71 days. The piece argues that the war has been catastrophic both militarily and economically, causing extensive damage to US bases in Gulf States, Israel, and regional infrastructure. The conflict escalated following guidance from Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Mossad Director David Barnea, who reportedly convinced Trump that Iran would quickly collapse. Contrary to expectations, Iran retaliated effectively, including shutting down the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a global oil crisis and potential worldwide economic downturn. The author questions Trump’s motivations, suggesting possible influences from campaign contributions, personal scandals, or ideological alignment with Israeli policies. The article urges Trump to reconsider his allegiance to Netanyahu, halt the Iran war, and rethink US military support for Israel until

item.title: Former GOP insider warns about Trump's behavior on Truth Social
item.description: After a late-night series of posts on Truth Social, former GOP insider George Conway expressed serious concern over Donald Trump's behavior. On Molly Jong-Fast's podcast, Conway compared Trump's online activity to the intense and desperate scenes from the film 'Downfall,' which depicts Adolf Hitler's last days. Conway argued that Trump's actions reflect the tendencies of a cornered narcissistic leader, describing them as increasingly desperate, delusional, and potentially dangerous. During Monday night, Trump made over 50 posts that criticized former Presidents Obama and Biden, reiterated unsubstantiated claims about the 2020 election, highlighted his renovations of the Reflecting Pool, and even imagined himself on U.S. currency. Conway warned that this pattern could escalate further and emphasized that the country cannot withstand a prolonged period of such behavior. The comparison to 'Downfall' underscores the severity with which Conway views Trump's recent activity, framing it as a

item.title: Alex Shuper Illustrates Human Footprints on Landscapes Through 3D Art
item.description: Latvian artist Alex Shuper has created a striking series of hyper-realistic 3D digital renderings that examine the profound effects humans have on the natural environment. His work uses landscapes as a canvas, incorporating human fingerprints, handprints, and footprints into the design of natural scenes. From lakes shaped like human feet to patches of wildflowers forming footprints, these imaginative visualizations blend art and environmental commentary, prompting viewers to consider how humanity leaves its mark on the planet. Published on Moss and Fog, the article highlights Shuper's prolific output and his ability to explore diverse themes and artistic styles. Through his work, Shuper not only showcases technical skill but also evokes reflection on the interconnection between people and nature, illustrating how human presence is both visible and transformative in the landscapes around us.

item.title: Concerns About the Future of Professional Journalism
item.description: The article reflects on the state of professional journalism and its challenges in today’s media landscape. The author, Noel Wauchope, emphasizes that while all journalism carries some bias, transparency about these biases is important. Good journalists strive to present facts while acknowledging complexity, cultural context, and human perspectives. The piece highlights the recent concerns expressed by renowned journalist Christiane Amanpour about changes in major media organizations like CNN and CBS under new ownership, suggesting that such shifts could undermine journalistic independence and objectivity. Amanpour’s reporting style exemplifies rigorous questioning, respect, and a humanitarian outlook, which the author values highly. The article also addresses the role of alternative media in maintaining press freedom, particularly in Australia’s highly adversarial legal and political system. It underscores the importance of nuanced commentary, recognition of multiple perspectives, and

item.title: Trump criticizes media coverage of Iran war, calling reports false and harmful
item.description: On May 12, 2026, President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social accusing media outlets of committing 'virtual treason' for reporting that Iran is performing well in the ongoing war. Trump stated that such coverage gives Iran false hope and described journalists as 'American cowards' who are rooting against the country. This statement comes amidst stalled U.S.-Iran negotiations. Analysts and commentators responded critically, noting that Trump's history with classified documents undermines his authority to make such claims. Several observers highlighted that, objectively, Iran's performance in the conflict, given the disparity in military and economic power, is accurate reporting. Critics argued that pressuring media to present a false narrative does not change reality. The episode reflects Trump's ongoing confrontational stance toward the press and his framing of criticism as disloyalty, while sparking widespread discussion about media freedom and accountability in wartime reporting.

item.title: Ben Shahn’s 1938 Photographs Document Daily Life in Ohio
item.description: In 1938, renowned photographer Ben Shahn traveled across Central Ohio for the Farm Security Administration (FSA) to document the lives of residents during the final years of the Great Depression. His work offers an intimate glimpse into everyday life in towns like London, Marysville, and Circleville, as well as the rural landscapes of Pickaway and Madison Counties. Shahn captured candid street scenes, portraying locals socializing, shopping, and engaging in ordinary activities. Iconic series such as “Saturday Afternoon” in London, Ohio, reveal the rhythms of small-town life, including men in overalls on street corners and shoppers outside storefronts. Beyond public spaces, Shahn documented private moments, including farm work, auctions, and scenes along U.S. Route 40, showing both the beauty and challenges of rural infrastructure. He frequently used a right-angle viewfinder on his Leica camera, allowing him to take unposed, natural portraits of people without disrupting their environme

item.title: How Supertramp’s 'Breakfast in America' Album Cover Was Created
item.description: Supertramp's 1979 album 'Breakfast in America' features one of the most inventive and iconic album covers of the 1970s. Designed to resemble a view of Lower Manhattan from an airplane window, the cover creatively uses everyday breakfast and diner items to construct the skyline. Actress and model Kate Murtagh portrays 'Libby,' a cheerful waitress standing in for the Statue of Liberty, holding orange juice as a torch and a menu as the tablet. The concept was developed by art director Mike Doud, designed by Mick Haggerty, and photographed by Aaron Rapoport. The back cover complements the front, showing the band enjoying breakfast in a real diner while being served by Libby, reinforcing the album’s playful take on American culture. The design earned Mike Doud a Grammy for Best Recording Package in 1980. Despite later conspiracy theories connecting the cover to the events of 9/11, the band and designers have clarified that any perceived resemblance was purely coincidental. The cover remains

item.title: The 1948 Healey Westland Roadster: British Engineering and Design Excellence
item.description: The 1948 Healey 2.4-Litre Westland Roadster represents a remarkable achievement in post-war British automotive engineering. Designed by Donald Healey, this hand-built sports car was among the fastest production vehicles of its era. Its robust Riley-sourced 2.4-litre engine, combined with a lightweight aluminum body, offered exceptional performance and agility. The car’s design, crafted initially at the Westland English Works, features elegant, flowing lines and distinctive sweeping wings, giving it a timeless aesthetic that exudes both speed and sophistication. Beyond its engineering feats, the Westland Roadster symbolized a renewed sense of freedom and innovation in the years following World War II. Its open-top design and handcrafted detailing emphasized personal expression and technical skill, setting the stage for the iconic Healey legacy that would influence sports car design for decades. Today, the 2.4-Litre Westland remains a celebrated example of craftsmanship, performance, and

item.title: Trump defends expansion of White House ballroom amid rising costs
item.description: During a press gaggle outside the White House on May 12, 2026, President Donald Trump reacted angrily when questioned about the escalating costs of a planned $1 billion ballroom at the White House. He insisted that the project remained under budget and ahead of schedule, despite proposals from Republican lawmakers to allocate additional funds for its construction. Trump emphasized that the size of the ballroom had been doubled to meet the needs of the White House, and repeatedly called reporters 'dumb' and 'stupid' during the exchange. He also noted that the final cost would depend on the finishes chosen for the project. The incident highlights ongoing scrutiny of White House expenditures and Trump’s confrontational style when responding to challenging questions from the media. The president’s remarks attracted widespread attention for their tone and the apparent disconnect between the announced donations and the congressional funding required. This coverage is part of broader reportin

item.title: Historical Overview of Alcohol Served to French Schoolchildren Before 1956
item.description: Until 1956, it was common in France for children to be served wine, beer, or cider during school lunch. These beverages were considered nutritional staples, believed to aid digestion, provide energy, and strengthen young bodies. In some cases, children were allowed up to half a liter a day. Early 20th-century beliefs about alcohol included its antibacterial properties, which were thought to prevent illness, and in areas with unsafe water, diluted wine was considered a safer option. Many parents supported the practice, often sending their children with bottles of wine or cider. The French Ministry of National Education, under Prime Minister Pierre Mendès France, officially banned alcohol for children under 14 in August 1956, recommending warm milk with a sugar cube instead. Students over 14 were still allowed limited alcohol consumption with parental consent. It was not until 1981, under President François Mitterrand, that all alcoholic beverages were banned for all schoolchildren, repl

item.title: Labour Suggests Reducing Employee National Insurance to Appeal to Voters
item.description: The news article reports on a Labour Party proposal aimed at regaining voter support by reducing employee National Insurance contributions. According to the party's recent report, the policy intends to increase disposable income for workers and provide financial relief, potentially boosting public approval. The suggestion reflects Labour's ongoing strategy to focus on cost-of-living issues and demonstrate responsiveness to citizens' economic concerns. Analysts note that such a reduction could have broader implications for government revenues and funding for public services, sparking debate among economists and policymakers. While the report is being discussed in political circles, critics warn about the feasibility and long-term effects of implementing such a measure, emphasizing the need for balanced fiscal planning. This approach is part of Labour’s wider campaign efforts to address economic pressures and influence voter behavior ahead of upcoming elections. The proposal underscores

item.title: Robert E. Cornish and His Experimental Resuscitation Research in the 1930s
item.description: Robert Edwin Cornish, born in 1903 in California, was a prodigious scientist who, by the early 1930s, became fascinated with reversing clinical death. He believed that if circulation and oxygenation were restored quickly, death might be reversible. Cornish developed a unique method involving a large see-saw-like tilting table to artificially circulate blood in bodies whose hearts had stopped. Subjects were strapped to the table, which was rocked back and forth, while he injected stimulants such as epinephrine, anticoagulants, and oxygenated saline. Cornish initially attempted human resuscitation but later focused on dogs, claiming to revive multiple animals, though often with severe neurological impairments. His experiments drew both sensational media coverage and public criticism over animal treatment, eventually forcing him out of university labs. He appeared in the 1935 film 'Life Returns,' portraying his work. Later, he sought to experiment on a freshly executed human, which was de

item.title: Los Angeles Chief Heat Officer Position Vacant Amid Leadership Changes
item.description: Los Angeles recently terminated Marta Segura, the city’s first Chief Heat Officer, leaving the Climate Emergency Mobilization Office with only one remaining employee. Segura, who took the role in 2022 under then-Mayor Eric Garcetti, was tasked with addressing extreme heat events, expanding cooling strategies, and advancing climate resilience. Her appointment made Los Angeles the third U.S. city to have a chief heat officer, following Phoenix and Miami. No reason was provided for her termination, though officials indicated that a replacement will be appointed soon to continue the work on Mayor Karen Bass’s Climate Action Plan. Segura’s departure is part of a broader trend of environmental leadership changes in the city, including resignations from other sustainability and clean energy advocates. Despite these personnel shifts, some city council members are continuing efforts to tackle heat-related issues, such as establishing indoor heat standards for renters. The Climate Emergency Mobi

item.title: Mary Astor's Early Acting Career and Iconic 1920s Portraits
item.description: Mary Astor, born Lucile Vasconcellos Langhanke in 1906, was an American actress who began her career as a teenager. Her family supported her ambitions, allowing her to take on small roles in films such as 'Sentimental Tommy' and early features like 'The Beggar Maid' and 'John Smith.' Her breakthrough came in 1924 when she starred alongside John Barrymore in 'Beau Brummel,' sparking a notable off-screen romantic relationship with the actor. Astor continued to appear in silent films throughout the 1920s at major studios like Paramount and Warner Bros., often portraying ingenues or romantic leads. Key films during this era include 'Don Q, Son of Zorro,' 'Two Arabian Knights,' 'Rose of the Golden West,' 'Dressed to Kill,' and 'Dry Martini.' With the advent of talkies, her deep voice initially led to some criticism, causing a temporary dip in her career around 1929. However, she successfully transitioned into more sophisticated roles as she entered her twenties. Astor was also recognized as

item.title: María Corda: A Silent Film Star's Journey from Europe to Hollywood
item.description: María Corda, born Mária Antónia Farkas on May 4, 1898, was a prominent Hungarian actress during the silent film era, celebrated for her beauty and commanding screen presence. In the late 1920s, she was transitioning from European stardom to Hollywood fame, moving to Beverly Hills in 1926 with her husband, director Alexander Korda, after signing a contract with First National. Her most notable Hollywood film was 'The Private Life of Helen of Troy' (1927), which unfortunately did not achieve significant success. Like many silent film stars, Corda struggled with the transition to sound films, as her limited English and strong accent made it difficult for her to continue her career in Hollywood. By the end of the decade, her marriage with Korda ended in divorce, and her acting career had effectively concluded. Known as the 'Hungarian Garbo,' Corda was frequently featured in fashion and film magazines of the late 1920s, admired for her art deco glamour and her roles in epic historical films

item.title: Solène Lory’s Memorable Stage Moment with Michael Jackson in Munich, 1997
item.description: Solène Lory, a devoted French fan of Michael Jackson, experienced a defining moment of her life during the HIStory World Tour. At 18, she was brought on stage during the Munich concert on July 6, 1997, to participate in the performance of “You Are Not Alone.” Her heartfelt interaction with Michael, including hugging him, kneeling, and expressing her emotions, became one of the most memorable fan moments from the tour. Solène had been a fan since 1992 after attending the Dangerous Tour in Toulouse, and she made deliberate efforts to secure a front-row spot at multiple shows across Europe. Despite an earlier disappointment in Paris, she persisted and was finally selected in Munich. During the on-stage moment, she brought a teddy bear as a personal keepsake but forgot to give it to him. Security eventually escorted her off stage, and she later recalled the lingering scent of Michael on her belongings. The interaction was genuine and unscripted, although the tour routinely featured a fan f

item.title: Texas GOP Leaders Disagree on Gas Tax Suspension Following Trump's Proposal
item.description: President Donald Trump's proposal to temporarily suspend the federal gas tax, in response to rising fuel prices influenced by the war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, has created tension among prominent Texas Republicans. The conflict is particularly visible between Governor Greg Abbott and outgoing Texas Agriculture Commissioner Sid Miller, who recently lost his primary election. Miller publicly urged Abbott to suspend the state gas tax as well, citing similar actions by governors in Indiana, Georgia, and Utah to provide relief to citizens facing higher gasoline prices. Abbott, through his press secretary, dismissed Miller's calls, stating that the governor has no legal authority to suspend state taxes and criticized Miller's credibility, referencing his electoral loss and past controversies. This political disagreement unfolds in a state known for its large oil production and traditionally low gas prices, adding further weight to the debate over tax relief. Meanwhile,

item.title: Kristi Noem vacates Coast Guard residence previously occupied by four-star admiral
item.description: Former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has finally left a waterfront Coast Guard residence that previously housed Adm. Linda Fagan, the first woman to lead the U.S. Coast Guard. Fagan was reportedly given only three hours to vacate the home before Noem moved in. After being dismissed from her DHS role by President Donald Trump in March 2026, Noem was appointed as Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas, a Western Hemisphere security initiative. However, she remained in the Coast Guard housing for over two months before officially moving out, according to MS NOW producer Steve Benen. Noem was one of several Trump cabinet members, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, who have taken up residences typically reserved for high-ranking military officials. The delay in vacating the property was noted by media outlets such as The Wall Street Journal and confirmed on 'The Rachel Maddow Show.' The Coast Guard's current commandant, Adm. Kevin Lu

item.title: Raquel Torres: Pioneering Latina Star of Early Hollywood
item.description: Raquel Torres (1908–1987) was a Mexican-American actress who became one of the most memorable faces of late silent films and early talkies in Hollywood. She rose to fame at just 19 years old with her breakout role in the 1928 film 'White Shadows in the South Seas,' where her beauty and emotive acting quickly captivated audiences around the world. Often cast in tropical and adventurous roles, Torres was celebrated for her vibrant energy and screen presence. One of her most notable appearances was alongside the Marx Brothers in the classic comedy 'Duck Soup.' Despite a relatively short career in Hollywood, her impact on the industry was significant, particularly as one of the early Latina actresses to achieve international recognition. Beyond her filmography, Torres is remembered as a symbol of early Hollywood’s charm and innovation. Her legacy continues to inspire interest in the history of cinema and the contributions of Latina talent during the golden age of film. Vintage photographs

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Audrey Hepburn Filming War and Peace (1956)
item.description: In 1956, Audrey Hepburn took on the iconic role of Natasha Rostova in the epic adaptation of Tolstoy’s 'War and Peace,' directed by King Vidor. These 40 vintage portraits showcase Hepburn both in character and candid moments on set, highlighting her timeless elegance and the youthful energy she brought to the role. The photographs capture her in period-accurate costumes, including high-waisted Empire dresses and intricate hairstyles of the Napoleonic era, reflecting the film’s meticulous attention to historical detail. Away from the grandeur of the ballrooms and battlefield scenes, these behind-the-scenes images reveal Hepburn’s natural charm, poise, and dedication to her craft. They serve not only as a testament to her enduring legacy as a cinematic icon but also as a visual documentation of one of the most ambitious film productions of the 1950s. Her portrayal of Natasha Rostova remains celebrated for its grace, vulnerability, and depth, cementing her status as the perfect embodiment

item.title: Understanding the Hantavirus Outbreak on a Cruise Ship
item.description: More than 100 passengers on the MV Hondius cruise ship were recently evacuated due to a hantavirus outbreak, with 18 Americans placed in biocontainment units in Nebraska and Georgia. As of now, 11 cases have been reported, including three deaths, all among passengers or crew. The virus identified is the Andes virus, a rare type of hantavirus capable of person-to-person transmission, although it spreads primarily through exposure to contaminated rodent droppings or urine. Infectious disease expert Nicole Iovine explains that the Andes virus infects deep in the lungs, making it far less contagious than COVID-19, which infects both the upper and lower respiratory tracts. Symptoms can appear between five days to six weeks after exposure and start with flu-like signs before potentially developing into severe disease. Close, prolonged contact is generally required for transmission, and casual encounters, like sitting next to someone at a dining table, carry minimal risk. Good ventilation and

item.title: Epstein Survivor Criticizes DOJ for Failing to Protect Her Identity in Released Files
item.description: During a House Oversight Committee hearing, an Epstein survivor identified as 'Roza' spoke out against the U.S. Department of Justice for failing to protect her anonymity in the release of files related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Roza, whose name appeared over 500 times in the documents, expressed that powerful figures were redacted while her identity was exposed, leaving her feeling unsafe. She recounted her first encounter with French modeling scout Jean-Luc Brunel at age 18, who recruited her into modeling and facilitated her U.S. immigration, a process financed by Epstein. Roza described how the sexual abuse began while Epstein was serving an 18-month sentence for a previous child abuse conviction, with at least 40 minor victims identified by the FBI. She criticized the DOJ’s actions as deliberate, stating that revealing her name while protecting other influential individuals was a conscious choice. Representative Robert García labeled her testimony a 'horrific exam

item.title: Rep. Norma Torres Presses HUD Secretary Scott Turner on California Fire Relief Funding
item.description: During a House Appropriations subcommittee hearing on May 12, 2026, Rep. Norma Torres (D-CA) confronted Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Scott Turner over delays in disaster relief funding for Californians affected by the January 2025 Southern California fires. Torres repeatedly asked when the Trump administration would submit a disaster package, emphasizing the urgent needs of families whose homes and lives were devastated. Turner mentioned visiting affected communities but deflected direct answers, citing local leadership and prior funding issues. Torres stressed that California taxpayers contribute significantly more in federal taxes than they receive in federal funding and demanded a government response that prioritizes the state’s recovery needs. The exchange drew attention for Torres’ persistent and direct questioning, highlighting tensions over federal disaster relief and the Trump administration’s fiscal priorities. The interaction also touched on broader concerns, su

item.title: Michael and La Toya Jackson in New York: Rare Candid Photos from 1977
item.description: In 1977, Michael Jackson moved to New York City to film the musical movie The Wiz, in which he played the Scarecrow. During this time, he shared a Manhattan apartment with his sister La Toya Jackson.
